title: El Granada hike & lunch at Barbara's Fishtrap 🌲🐟
description:
Join us for a lovely hike and tasty lunch on **Saturday April 11th**!

We'll start by hiking the **South Ridge, Consolation Point View, and Middle Ridge Trail Loop** near El Granada. After we finish t
fullDescription:
Join us for a lovely hike and tasty lunch on **Saturday April 11th**!

We'll start by hiking the **South Ridge, Consolation Point View, and Middle Ridge Trail Loop** near El Granada. After we finish the hike we'll head to the **Barbara's Fishtrap** in Half Moon Bay (\~6 min drive away) for lunch.

**The Hike:** South Ridge, Consolation Point View, and Middle Ridge Trail Loop https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/south-ridge-trail-dolphine-fire-road-and-middle-ridge-trail-loop

The hike is 2.5 miles with a 'Hard' difficulty level, 662 ft elevation gain, a mix of sun and shade, and beautiful views. Some sections of the trail are flat and wide, while other sections are narrow, rugged and hilly - please wear sturdy shoes! The hike should take 1.5-2 hours. Bring sun protection, plenty of water, and snacks.

**Lunch:** Barbara's Fishtrap in Half Moon Bay https://www.barbarasfishtrap.com/
We'll meet up at Barbara's after the hike. There's usually a long wait to sit inside at lunchtime on the weekends, so we plan to order from the to-go counter and sit at the outdoor tables. Barbara's specializes in delicious fish and chips and other seafood. There are vegetarian options, but they are limited at the to-go counter. If a group wants to sit inside for the full menu/experience they are more than welcome to - just keep in mind that there will likely be a wait 😊. There are pretty places to stroll along the waterside while you wait for your table.

\* I've attached a picture of the outdoor menu - not sure if it's 100% up to date. You can see the full menu at their website.

**Timing:** We're meeting at **9:45am** and starting the hike at 10am sharp. Please leave yourself additional time for parking in case the lot is full and you need to park nearby.
**Meeting point #1 (hike trailhead) - 9:45am:** Quarry Park Parking Lot https://maps.app.goo.gl/sPQ1aPi8xSdspg8L7
\* Don't go to the trailhead listed on Alltrails, according to several users it's gated private land.
**Meeting point #2 (lunch) - approx. 12-12:30pm:** Barbara's Fishtrap - 281 Capistrano Rd, Half Moon Bay https://maps.app.goo.gl/P2nWqCm51t8nYXD98

Hope to see you all there!

fullDescription:

title: Wildcat Canyon San Pablo Ridge 7.2 Mile Moderate Hike
description: WAIVER: For us to hold our large events at local parks, the local parks require us to carry insurance for our events. To participate in our events, please sign this waiver (required by our insurance u
fullDescription:
WAIVER: For us to hold our large events at local parks, the local parks require us to carry insurance for our events. To participate in our events, please sign this waiver (required by our insurance underwriter) for each event. Please do so online prior to arriving (works on mobile):
https://www.sleeplateandhike.com/waiver-signing-form/

HIKING TIME: 3-3.5 hours

DISTANCE: 7.2 miles, Loop.

ELEVATION GAIN: Approx. 1400 feet

DIFFICULTY: Moderate terrain and elevation.

FACILITIES: Restrooms and water at trailhead.

RECOMMENDED MAPS:
[https://www.ebparks.org/sites/default/files/wildcat-canyon-brochure.pdf](https://www.ebparks.org/sites/default/files/wildcat-canyon-brochure.pdf)

[https://www.alltrails.com/explore/trail/us/california/wildcat-creek-trail-havey-creek-trail-san-pablo-ridge-trail-and-belgum-trail](https://www.alltrails.com/explore/trail/us/california/wildcat-creek-trail-havey-creek-trail-san-pablo-ridge-trail-and-belgum-trail)

MEETING PLACE:
Parking lot at trailhead. Free. 66 spots available. Some street parking available as well. Meet by the bathrooms.

• Please use the Google Maps link below to familiarize yourself with the trip and explore various route options from your home. Zoom in all the way.
• The GPS locations below and the map link above are accurate enough to find us by looking around when you get there.

GPS Degrees Decimal Format:
37.951920, -122.317232
https://goo.gl/maps/JD4SUuFZeJNPdiDP8

If you have the Waze app installed on your phone, click this link. It will open Waze and start navigating to the trailhead.
waze://?ll=37.951920,-122.317232&navigate=yes

• As always, it is good practice to confirm the night before that any app you use takes you to where you expect by comparing the destination with what is posted above on your PC/Mac
• Most street addresses provided are approximate, and may be up to a couple of miles from the hike location. Use GPS coordinates when driving, if possible.

CARPOOLING:
We will use this link for carpooling. Please sign up here.
https://www.groupcarpool.com/t/w4t8ig

Please refrain from trying to negotiate your carpool in the comments, as it is equivalent to sending multiple "Reply All" emails to everyone. Please start your carpool quest, by first adding yourself to the carpool link above. Then, use the contact info provided by the driver or rider in the link above to contact them directly and privately. Please be considerate of others and resist the urge to "Reply All". Thank you for understanding.

SUMMARY:
Wildcat Canyon Regional Park encompasses 2,427 acres along the Wildcat Creek watershed and the surrounding hills and ridges. The park extends from the Tilden Nature Area above the Berkeley hills in the south to historic Alvarado Park at the north end in Richmond.
https://www.ebparks.org/parks/wildcat/

HIKING DIRECTIONS:
Starting at Alvarado Staging Area, head northeast on Wildcat Creek Trail for 0.23 miles. Pass Bonita Trail on your left and continue straight on Wildcat Creek Trail for 0.21 miles. Pass Belgium Trail on your left and continue straight on Wildcat Creek Trail for 1.57 miles. Pass Mezue Trail on your left and continue straight for 0.10 miles. Pass Leonards Trail on your left and continue straight for 0.18 miles. Cross a creek and pass Rifle Range Road Trail on your right (0.04 miles), then take Havey Canyon Trail on your left for 1.50 miles. Turn left onto Nimitz Way (paved road) for 0.68 miles. Turn left onto San Pablo Ridge Trail for 0.07 miles, pass Mezue Trail on your left again, and continue straight/right on San Pablo Ridge Trail for 1.21 miles. Pass Green Ridge Trail on your right and continue for 0.15 miles. At the four-way intersection, continue straight onto Belgum Trail for 0.20 miles. Pass Monte Cresta Trail on your right and continue straight/left on Belgum Trail for 0.65 miles. Turn right back onto Wildcat Creek Trail for 0.21 miles, then pass Bonita Trail on your right and continue for 0.23 miles back to the trailhead.

PLEASE READ THE EVENT DESCRIPTION ABOVE BEFORE POSTING A MESSAGE.

title: Korean for Everyone - Beginners: Free Class & Cultural Meetup
description:
**Come hang out with us at the San Francisco Korean Culture and Language Meetup Group!**

This Meetup is for **beginner-level Korean learners**.
If you already have a basic understanding of Korean and
fullDescription:
**Come hang out with us at the San Francisco Korean Culture and Language Meetup Group!**

This Meetup is for **beginner-level Korean learners**.
If you already have a basic understanding of Korean and want to focus more on **speaking fluently and improving expression**, please sign up for the **Intermediate Level** class instead.

In our last session, we learned how to form the past tense in Korean. For your review, here is a simple summary of the past tense forms.

In Korean, the past tense is usually made by adding:
–았어요 (used when the last vowel of the verb stem is 아 or 오)
–었어요 (used with all other verb stems)
–했어요 (used with all verbs that end in 하다)

Examples:
저는 집에 갔어요.
→ I went home.
저는 커피를 마셨어요.
→ I drank coffee.
저는 어제 공부를 했어요.
→ I studied yesterday.

Vowel Sounds
👉 https://youtube.com/shorts/cNpBn5vFmas?si=_4iMeM01Tv0CMfRZ

Combined Vowel Sounds
👉https://youtube.com/shorts/bFM4dQIEzfE?si=LMeRs1OPMoncUQWY

Consonant sounds
👉 https://youtube.com/shorts/5huk-375qTM?si=HKLK-h-aeLyeV_dX

Combination of vowel and consonant chart
👉 https://youtu.be/ihtB9EVOfpg?si=AYfyYVJPyIJ0fY-l

**For the Intermediate Level, please RSVP separately for that class. We have opened another Intermediate Korean class on Meetup.**
Address: 1911 Hayes Street
Time: 2:30pm (Intermediate Level)

See you all!

title: English Country Dance Marin
description: English country dancing is done in long lines (like contra), circles, or in geometric sets (squares, triangles). Like our contra dances, there is live music and a caller. You can come by yourself or w
fullDescription:
English country dancing is done in long lines (like contra), circles, or in geometric sets (squares, triangles). Like our contra dances, there is live music and a caller. You can come by yourself or with friends, since we change partners after each dance. If you’ve ever done contra or square dancing, much of our dance vocabulary will be familiar to you since both of those styles evolved from English country dancing.

Admission is $15 for members of NBCDS and its affiliates.

title: Werewolf Potluck
description: New players always welcome, we are friendly and can teach you [how to play](https://docs.google.com/document/d/10hhAtoAWkwjAOFjkcYgia2Dm0Im1M3Vr2DwvC7hu9uE/edit?tab=t.0) including our [7 house rules](
fullDescription:
New players always welcome, we are friendly and can teach you [how to play](https://docs.google.com/document/d/10hhAtoAWkwjAOFjkcYgia2Dm0Im1M3Vr2DwvC7hu9uE/edit?tab=t.0) including our [7 house rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-W7kjNNWEmFptS6uz3jQAKLP_ZZJ1ntYECg0GTdAXCo/edit?fbclid=IwY2xjawMPQONleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETFlNks3Y3ZCNTU2R0p2OVBFAR4O3SLi5Cqapu5FIQxkyu2XCRfVQWRL96uix08e3hbapt7ZBAUCGDOdFLXAIQ_aem_27HEyDvk1X1alLK8co7hLw&tab=t.0) that make it such a fun and unique experience that you will love! Adults only. Bring your friends, but RSVP required for each guest. **Please bring some food or drink to share so you can stay and play all day!**

Arrive anytime and join the next village. We run games in two rooms, so the dead can join whichever game starts next. We host at our home in Brisbane. We have a dog who will be in daycare and a cat who will be in your lap if you let her. Keep shoes on inside.

***Join our Facebook group where members host unofficial events***
(https://www.facebook.com/groups/werewolfsf/)

**Village Guidelines:**
• No insults, No criticism after game, Share the floor
• Don't make it personal, don't take it personal

**Moderator Guidelines:**
• Enforce rules but be light-hearted.
• Use our house rules (and play with us before moderating)
• Get pre-approval on any experimental roles

***Please update your RSVP (including wait list) if you can't make it.*** *Late cancels, no shows, and crashers without RSVP take spots away from others, so repeat offenders will be removed from our group*
group: Werewolf Players of SF Bay Area

title: (Happy Hour and early Dinner) PABU Izakaya:  Table for 6.  (Deposit $50)
description:
[PABU](https://theminagroup.com/restaurants/pabu-izakaya/) is a Mina Group Japanese restaurant that also has a secret Japanese whisky cocktail menu hidden in a bottle behind the Bar.

This vibrant res
fullDescription:
[PABU](https://theminagroup.com/restaurants/pabu-izakaya/) is a Mina Group Japanese restaurant that also has a secret Japanese whisky cocktail menu hidden in a bottle behind the Bar.

This vibrant restaurant blends expertly crafted sushi, sashimi, and robata with a dynamic atmosphere, making it ideal for casual dining or special occasions. With a commitment to premium ingredients and innovative flavors, they promise an unforgettable culinary adventure.

**Administrative Trivia**

Dinner is expected to run around $125 (minus tax, tip, health mandate).

Happy Hour can run $25-$50 for cocktail and shared small plates. At around 5:30pm, we'll move to our dinner reservation.

[Happy Hour menu](http://mx.michaelmina.net/recipe-exchange/download/?file_id=36803&nodl=true)
[Dinner menu](http://mx.michaelmina.net/recipe-exchange/download/?file_id=37194&nodl=true)

**Note this is ala carte dinner and NOT the $195 Omakase unless every attendee votes for the Omakase ...**

Everyone starts on the waitlist and first come first deposit will be moved to the attending.

**Taxes and Fees**

Dining in San Francisco has a sales tax (8.625%) and possibly an employee health mandate (5%), a tip (20%) may be automatically added because of the size of our party, even on individual bills. I usually add \~35% to each raw bill adding individual dishes, which should include all of these, and round final payment to the closest dollar.

Please bring cash, Apple Cash, or use Zelle.

**Parking and Transportation**

BART: Embarcadero Station
Muni: 1, 30X
Street parking: difficult but possible
Lot parking: [Embarcadero 2 garage](https://embarcaderocenter.com/parking/).

title: Second Saturday of the Month - Live and In Person #31 -- PURPLE BOOK!
description:
THIS IS AN IN-PERSON MEETUP!

**We continue with our rotation of playing from only one of the three Daily Ukulele Books each month. This month we'll play from the Daily Ukulele - Another Year book (th
fullDescription:
THIS IS AN IN-PERSON MEETUP!

**We continue with our rotation of playing from only one of the three Daily Ukulele Books each month. This month we'll play from the Daily Ukulele - Another Year book (the Purple Book).**

If you'd like to play a song that isn't in the Purple Book, please let me know in advance so I can post a link to the song chart on our meetup page, and please bring additional copies with you.

If you don’t have the Yellow, Blue, or Purple Books yet, you can purchase them from Jim Beloff’s website [https://www.fleamarketmusic.com/store/scripts/prodList.asp?idCategory=24].
You can also find them on amazon.com and other online bookstores, on eBay, and at local music shops and bookstores. Please bring your instrument(s), a floor or table music stand, your singing voices, and your fabulous ukulele spirit!

We'll play music from **5:00 pm to 7:30 pm**, with a short intermission.

We'll go around the room so that everyone can pick songs to play. If you pick a song, you will be encouraged (but not required) to lead it. If you'd like to let the group know in advance which songs you plan to select, please post the songs you choose in the comments, and we'll play one of those songs each time it's your turn to pick a song.

The dropbox link to The Daily Ukulele.
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/uca82l70r9a4hgjqohsgq/Daily_ukulele_yellow_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=44gm13r5gbvhstiv7ibnuojt1&st=dvrcg7n4&dl=0
The dropbox link to the Daily Ukulele - Leap Year Edition:
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/hy9pnffip1lc1rxs5zc8q/Daily_Ukulele_blue_Leap_Year_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=oug9yq9ezckyly3ekj3su539u&st=2hgndbig&dl=0
The Dropbox link to the Groovy Song Book used in the Bronco Billy Zoom jams:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/ezywmwbck8229gu/2021-05-22_v9_Official%20BBJ%20Virtual%20Song%20Packet.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/ezywmwbck8229gu/2021-05-22_v9_Official%20BBJ%20Virtual%20Song%20Packet.pdf?dl=0)

A voluntary donation of $5 to defray Meetup website costs and to tip our hosts is appreciated. San Francisco Ukulele Rebellion logo kazoos will be available for purchase.

**IMPORTANT NOTICE:**
**I do not disclose the location of our in-person jams on the Meetup website. While the date and time of these in-person meetups will still be posted here, I'll be notifying people of the location of these meetups using an email distribution list. *If you'd like to be on this list, please email me at* sfukerebellion@yahoo.com *from the email address you'd like me to add to the distribution list.***

***COVID UPDATE***: Covid and the flu have surged this winter. I think it's safe for us to meet, but ***I strongly encourage people to observe social distancing and other measures, like masking, that have been shown to inhibit the spread of airborne viruses like Covid and the flu***. For the safety of others in the group, ***please don't attend this meetup if you are not feeling well or if you are experiencing Covid-like or flu-like symptoms***. I won't require people to test for Covid, but ***I strongly encourage you to test for Covid on Saturday afternoon***. From what I understand, most of the people I know who have gotten Covid recently got it from someone who didn't even know they had it at the time of exposure. Thank you in advance for your thoughtfulness.

title: Quietly Queer at Church Of 8 Wheels
description: Let's get together and roller skate at the Church Of 8 Wheels Roller Disco in San Francisco! This is a low-pressure, come-as-you-are space for queer folks + allies to connect, have fun, and experience
fullDescription:
Let's get together and roller skate at the Church Of 8 Wheels Roller Disco in San Francisco! This is a low-pressure, come-as-you-are space for queer folks + allies to connect, have fun, and experience a unique activity with other people. There's no agenda here, no expectations. Please DM me if the event is full and I'll mark you down as attending (the RSVP is capped at 10 people by Meetup, but I cap it at 15).

**🗓 Date & Time:** April 11 at 7PM
**📍 Meeting Spot:** We'll gather in front of Church of 8 Wheels, 554 Fillmore St, San Francisco, CA 94117
**🎟️ Cost:** Tickets are $20 **+** roller skate rentals are $5. I would suggest you get your ticket in advance here (select Saturday session, then the corresponding date): http://www.churchof8wheels.com/
**💡 Vibe:** Come as you are! Be comfy, be stylish, be retro, however you prefer. There is no bar or food, just good music and disco lights!

title: House Concert Featuring Diana Gameroa
description: Please join me for my next house concert featuring Diana Gameros. This concert is 13 years in the making, a sweet story I'll share with you on the night of the show. I am incredibly honored to host th
fullDescription:
Please join me for my next house concert featuring Diana Gameros. This concert is 13 years in the making, a sweet story I'll share with you on the night of the show. I am incredibly honored to host this notable, celebrated Bay Area artist in my home! This will surely be a magical evening.

**Diana Gameros** is a singer, guitarist, pianist, composer, songwriter, lead teaching artist with the Lullaby Project, and a core member of the Immigrant Orchestra. Born and raised in Juárez, México, she immigrated to the United States as a teenager. Over the last decade in the Bay Area, she has released three albums featuring original songs in Spanish and English, as well as interpretations of Mexican classics; has been recognized multiple times by NPR’s Tiny Desk Contest; and was the subject of a documentary film released by KQED in 2020. Her music tells stories of family, migration, and home, and has led her to perform alongside prominent artists such as Joan Baez, Natalia Lafourcade, and the San Francisco Symphony. Diana received the 2023 Music Award Grant from the Gerbode Foundation to record her latest album, *Volver a la Luz* (Return to the light), with songs that acknowledge our personal and collective grief, but also inspire and invite us: to turn toward the sun, the stars, to remember the light already living within us and to let it guide us toward our true liberation and peace. On April 11, Diana will perform solo, on guitar, vocals, and piano.

Links: [website](https://ncls1.com/irk?o=feei03&s=688952&subid=evite&bkd=dianagameros.com&sp=018AIIAE4SKDAA2SYEPRCB73BMQXBA&di=ab1d6432-5106-4343-97c5-003795f02620&d=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.dianagameros.com), [Latest album](https://ncls1.com/irk?o=feei03&s=688952&subid=evite&bkd=bandcamp.com&sp=018AIIAE4SKDAA2SYEPRCB73BMQXBA&di=321abbb0-1083-4ef7-9f9a-7acb683fe2f2&d=https%3A%2F%2Fdianagameros.bandcamp.com), [KQED Arts Documentary](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1IacYXIN5Uk)

Donations are $25 per person in cash at the door, or please Venmo the artist directly @dianagameros.

As is custom with house concerts, please bring a beverage, appetizer or dessert to share. But please, NO RED WINE to save my carpet from that after-show blush!

title: Glen Pearson Quintet
description: In what I consider to be a can't-miss show, pianist and educator Glen Pearson (winner of a 2024 Grammy for his work with the Count Basie Orchestra) heads up a quintet of excellent local jazz musicians
fullDescription:
In what I consider to be a can't-miss show, pianist and educator Glen Pearson (winner of a 2024 Grammy for his work with the Count Basie Orchestra) heads up a quintet of excellent local jazz musicians well known to Meetup regulars. They will be playing a hard-swinging cross-section of jazz standards and original pieces. The band members are:
Mike Olmos, trumpet
Sheldon Brown, saxophone
Gary Brown, bass
Sylvia Cuenca, drums

COST: About $26 for the concert ticket - plus a $1.00 donation to the Meetup organizer, if you haven't contributed recently to offsetting the hefty fees that Meetup charges me.

TICKETS: **Buy your own tickets.** Seating is general admission, first-come, first-served.

**TICKET LINK:**
https://concerts.jazzschool.org/pianolove-glen-pearsons-testament-quintet/

WHEN/WHERE TO MEET: Unless joining me for dinner beforehand, meet inside the performance area of the jazz school's DOWNSTAIRS location at **2087 Addison** by 7:00 p.m., when the doors open. so we can aim to get decent seats together. (The program consists of a pre-concert talk at 7:30 p.m., followed by a 90-minute concert, running from 8:00 pm to 9:30 p.m..)

OPTIONAL DINNER BEFORE THE SHOW: At 6:00 p.m. at Tender Greens on University near Shattuck.

HOW TO FIND ME: I'm WF, about 5 feet tall, with short salt-and-pepper hair and glasses. (See my photo with my Meetup profile.) I'll send my cellphone number to RSVPers prior to the event. If I'm missing anyone when I get seated, I'll post a Comment on the Meetup Event Page with my location.

TRANSPORTATION/PARKING: The venue is less than a half-block from the downtown Berkeley BART station, as well as several AC Transit bus lines on Shattuck and University. There's a paid garage in the same block as the jazz school, or you can typically find free street parking as you get away from Shattuck or go north of University.

MORE INFO:

title: The Painted Ladies & Cherry Blossom Festival (Dogs Welcome)
venue: St Francis yacht club start of San Francisco Bay Trail
description: Near the Presidio Golden Gate National Recreation Area, we are going up several sets of historic and scenic staircases. along the edge of the Presidio past the lovely and majestic palace of fine arts,
fullDescription:
Near the Presidio Golden Gate National Recreation Area, we are going up several sets of historic and scenic staircases. along the edge of the Presidio past the lovely and majestic palace of fine arts, we head up the steep and busy Lyon staircase to panoramic views of the San Francisco Bay. We wonder through mansions of Pacific Heights and move to Alamo Square Park and **“ The Painted Ladies”** or the **“Seven Sisters",** a row of colorful Victorian houses located at 710–720 Steiner Street. These Queen Anne-style homes built in the19th-century are one of the most well-recognized examples of California Victorian architecture, then down to Japantown and The Northern California Cherry Blossom Festival. The festival features traditional Japanese music, martial arts demonstrations, tea ceremonies, and a Cherry Blossom Queen Program that’s been running for five decades. Cultural exhibitions include origami displays, bonsai presentations, and calligraphy demonstrations.

**Level:** Intermediate Plus
**Total Distance:** 8.5+ miles
**Hiking Time:** 4 - 4.5+ hours
**Elevation Change:** 322 stairs Lyon Street, 200 sand stairs Baker beach
Parking fee: Free Parking lot, leashed Dogs allowed, standard restroom and water.

**GPS:** Latitude: N37.805715° Longitude: W 122.44912°

**Map:** https://www.sfmta.com/sites/default/files/pressreleases/2015/Muni%20Map%202015.pdf

**Trailhead / Meeting Point:** Starting point, next to the beach near St Francis Yacht Club Parking Lot 99 Yacht Road, San Francisco, 94123, Parking on both sides of the building.

**Hiking Directions: Starting point,** beach near St Francis Yacht Club Parking Lot 99 Yacht Road,, then back and cross the street and walk past the Palace of fine arts, then cross Lombard, Up Lyon street and continue on the steep staircase to the top Broadway street view, Left on Broadway, Right at Scott Street Alta Plaza, then continue on Scott and enter Alamo Square Park view the **Painted Ladies and city views, (Group photo and break)** Left on Steiner, check out part of Filmore street, then Right on Post street and enter Japantown and **Cherry Blossom Festival**, so at this point everyone will proceed on their own and if they want to meet at Fillmore and Post at specific time or not that if fine...

**Driving Directions:**
From Highway 101 north head into San Francisco, stay left on North 101 towards Golden gate bridge, Turn Right onto Market Street, then Left onto Franklin Street, Left onto Lombard St, then Right onto Broderick Street, Left onto Marina Blvd with a slight right onto Old Mason Street Left into the Little Marina green and Yacht Club road, Parking on both sides of Yacht club.

**Carpooling Link:** (Sign-up here)
https://www.groupcarpool.com/t/huz8p7

**Carpooling Procedures:**
No carpool discussion in the comments, join the carpool link and contact the driver or rider directly.

**Thank you .......**

**Legal Disclaimer:** Under what we are about Section
[https://www.meetup.com/Urban-and-Bay-Hikers/](https://www.meetup.com/Urban-and-Bay-Hikers/)

title: The Presidio-History, Architecture & Tunnel Tops Walk
description: A very nice walk through the Presidio of San Francisco, starting in Pacific Hts.. As we take an enjoyable walk through the grounds of the Presidio we'll learn about major points in its colorful histor
fullDescription: A very nice walk through the Presidio of San Francisco, starting in Pacific Hts.. As we take an enjoyable walk through the grounds of the Presidio we'll learn about major points in its colorful history. For instance did you know that in 1899 during the Spanish/Philipine War thousands of very young, raw American recruits from all over the country where stationed in tents bordering fashionable Pacific Hts. Here they took US Army basic training and awaited deployment to the Philippine War. It was not a quiet time there, or nearby to say the least.

description:

*Most social gatherings end with a handful of shallow pleasantries and no real connection
fullDescription:
**RSVP via:**
https://app.gistirl.com/event-details/vi2HI54sCLYV7zFzSjhQ
**FREE for Early Bird | $ Later on**

*Most social gatherings end with a handful of shallow pleasantries and no real connection. This one ends differently.*

You will spend an hour in genuine one-on-one conversations with people seeking meaningful social connection beyond work and networking. There is no awkward mingling or wondering who to approach next. Instead of surface-level small talk and networking-style questions that lead to forgettable interactions, you pick your favorite people from the rounds and share a communal meal on a giant blanket area and use conversation cards to spark deep discussions. Same afternoon. No follow-up texts. No scheduling headaches.

Just real conversation that turns into a real plan — with people you already vibe with.

By the time the sun starts to set, you've shared a meal and real stories with a new group of friends who know the real you. Maybe this becomes your monthly deep-connection picnic crew. Maybe it's just a great afternoon. Either way, you're not doing it alone.

BYOB (blanket) & bring a dish to share. People bring blankets and we place them next to each other on the grass, creating one large communal picnic area.

Agenda

12:50PM - Check-In
Follow the link to complete a quick profile setup on your phone.

1:00PM - 2:00PM - One-on-One Rounds
Our app pairs you for focused conversations. Find out who people are as humans, their values, and what makes them tick, rather than what they do for work.

2:00PM+ - Form Groups & place your blankets together and start the first round of conversation cards
Reconnect with your favorite matches, form groups of 4-6, and place your blankets together and start the first round of conversation cards. Your group leaves together - tonight.

Don't just collect contacts. Actually connect deeply over a shared picnic with new people and see if this becomes your regular crew for future deep-dive dinners and meaningful social outings.

Want to stay in touch with someone? Use the app - it lets you connect without ever sharing your phone number.

title: Sunday Prime Rib Dinner at Lolinda: Table for 6 ($25 deposit)
description:
SF Foodies frequently visit some of San Francisco’s better known prime rib houses. But in the last year there is a new player in the prime rib market – Argentinian steak house Lolinda.

Lolinda only o
fullDescription:
SF Foodies frequently visit some of San Francisco’s better known prime rib houses. But in the last year there is a new player in the prime rib market – Argentinian steak house Lolinda.

Lolinda only offers their prime rib dinner on Sunday, and it must be pre-ordered in advance. I pre-ordered 6 prime rib dinners. So while you can order additional items from the menu, you must order the prime rib.

Chefs Alejandro Morgan and Juan Torres work together to create a curated menu that combines Latin American flavors with California sensibilities. The prime rib is slow roasted and expertly carved, served with side dishes that rotate seasonally. The dinner, including sides, is priced at $68 per person.

You will also be able to choose from inventive Argentine-inspired cold and hot appetizers and desserts, as well as an extensive cocktail and wine menu.

**Costs**
Everyone is responsible for the food and drinks they order, unless the group decides to order shared items and split the cost. No separate checks. Please be prepared to reimburse the host via cash, Venmo, Zelle, or Apple Pay. The prime rib entree will be $68 ($91 with tax, mandate, and tip).

The $25 deposit covers the restaurant’s no-show fee. I will reach out to collect it via Venmo, Zelle, or Apple Pay about a week before the event. If you attend, it will be deducted from your bill.

**Taxes and Fees**
Dining in San Francisco has a sales tax (8.625%). The restaurant adds a 5% employee health surcharge and we will include a 20% gratuity.

**Parking and Transportation**
Muni 14, 49 to Mission & 22nd
BART to 24th & Mission
Mission Bartlett Garage, 3255 21st St.

title: Zion and Bryce Hiking and Glamping
description: Unreal hoodoos? Check. Slot canyons? Majestic overlooks? Check. All this and more (like heated tents with electricity) await you during our Women’s Hiking and Glamping in Zion National Park and Bryce
fullDescription:
Unreal hoodoos? Check. Slot canyons? Majestic overlooks? Check. All this and more (like heated tents with electricity) await you during our Women’s Hiking and Glamping in Zion National Park and Bryce Canyon weekend adventure!

**BRYCE CANYON HIKING AND BEGINNER-FRIENDLY CANYONEERING**

Escape to prehistoric Utah where you’ll hike stupid-pretty trails in Zion National Park and Bryce Canyon National Park. Wade through the Virgin River below 1,500′ canyon walls in the Narrows. Spend an entire day exploring Bryce Canyon National Park. Ghost the tourists as you wander deep into orange hoodoos. Explore areas of Zion so remote, they can only be reached by rappelling, scrambling, and down-climbing on a beginner-friendly canyoneering route.

**ZION NATIONAL PARK’S SCOUT LOOKOUT**

Beastmaster up the West Rim Trail to Scout Lookout and enjoy some of the best views in Zion National Park. You’ll hike through Refrigerator Canyon, and shimmy up all 21 of Walters Wiggles to Scout Lookout with panoramic views of Zion Canyon and Virgin River. Afterward, dry your pits as you descend the trail to an easier hike around the scenic streams of Upper and Lower Emerald Pools.

**GLAMPING**

Every night you’ll rest your head in Insta-worthy glamping tents bordering Zion National Park. Kick it by the campfire under a diamond sky with goddesses who aren’t afraid to get dirty.

**See the website for full itinerary, details and to book.**
[https://explorerchick.com/adventures/hiking-glamping-zion-bryce-canyon](https://explorerchick.com/adventures/hiking-glamping-zion-bryce-canyon)

title: Florence’s Crown Jewels : Palazzo Vecchio, the Uffizi & the Accademia
description:
**REGISTRATION REQUIRED!** **[Click here to register](https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/9517747166845/WN_YJQ1nVjvRnqyuUrRb8cUrA)**

Step into the heart of Florence and discover the artistic tre
fullDescription:
**REGISTRATION REQUIRED!** **[Click here to register](https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/9517747166845/WN_YJQ1nVjvRnqyuUrRb8cUrA)**

Step into the heart of Florence and discover the artistic treasures that shaped the Renaissance. In this immersive virtual tour, we’ll explore three of the city’s crown jewels: Palazzo Vecchio, the Uffizi Gallery, and the Accademia.
We begin at Palazzo Vecchio, the fortress-palace that has watched over Florence for more than seven centuries. From its grand courtyard to the majestic Hall of the Five Hundred, you’ll uncover the stories of the Medici, political intrigues, and the masterpieces hidden within its walls.
Next, we move to the Uffizi Gallery, one of the world’s greatest museums. Through beautifully curated slides, we’ll explore iconic works by Botticelli, Leonardo, Michelangelo, Raphael, and Caravaggio. You’ll learn how these masterpieces transformed art forever and why the Uffizi remains the beating heart of the Renaissance.
Finally, we arrive at the Accademia Gallery, home to the most famous sculpture on earth: Michelangelo’s David. We’ll unravel the genius behind this extraordinary masterpiece, explore Michelangelo’s unfinished “Prisoners,” and understand how Florence became the cradle of artistic revolution.
This virtual experience blends history, art and legends guiding you through Florence’s most iconic landmarks without the crowds — just beauty, insight, and the magic of the Renaissance.
Join us to celebrate Florence’s timeless brilliance and immerse yourself in its masterpieces from the comfort of home.

**HOW TO JOIN:**
The tour will be hosted on Zoom. To register, you are **required to enter your full name** (first and last name).After the registration, you will receive via email a personal link to access the virtual event. To protect your privacy, your camera will be disabled during the event.
The tour will last approximately 1 hour. At the end of the tour you will be given the chance to interact and make questions just like a live tour. The tour takes place in English!
**PRICE**: **How much does the virtual tour cost?**
Our virtual tours are **tip-based** and not free, they rely entirely on contributions from participants. This means we do not impose a fixed ticket price to join. Every participant is welcome to take part in the tour and decide how much to contribute based on their satisfaction and personal budget. **You can leave a tip here:** [https://nextstop-italy.com/donation/](https://nextstop-italy.com/donation/)
❗**Please note**❗: To ensure the sustainability of the project, we regularly monitor the donations received, based on participation in previous tours, and, if necessary, we may apply participation limits or suspend accounts.This is essential to maintain the quality of our tours, ensure fairness, and continue rewarding the valuable work of our guides. *If you register for a tour but cannot attend live or watch the recording, we kindly ask you to unsubscribe before the tour begins. Otherwise, your registration will still be counted as participation.*

**Can’t watch our virtual tour live** due to the time zone or are you unable to join us live? Register at the virtual tour and we will send you the **recording** hence you can watch it in your own time! **Recordings are also tip-based**, as they support our cultural project and the work of our guides.
**Check out all our [Italy Virtual Tours](https://nextstop-italy.com/virtual-tours/)**
group: Italy Virtual Tours: Art, Culture and Beauty - San Francisco

title: FREE Unwind: Knit, Crochet, and Fiber Social @ Ace Makerspace
description:
YOU MUST RSVP/PURCHASE TICKETS THROUGH THE ACE MAKERSPACE WEBSITE: [www.acemakerspace.org/calendar](https://www.acemakerspace.org/calendar/)

Knit and crochet the night away in the Ace Textiles Studio
fullDescription:
YOU MUST RSVP/PURCHASE TICKETS THROUGH THE ACE MAKERSPACE WEBSITE: [www.acemakerspace.org/calendar](https://www.acemakerspace.org/calendar/)

Knit and crochet the night away in the Ace Textiles Studio! Unwind, work on projects, bring a snack to share, and socialize with others on fiber art adventures. This hosted event is largely self-directed, and while mentorship is available based on your existing skill set or current project, no one-on-one beginner lessons will be available. Attendees will also have access to a swift and ball winder for making pull-skeins.

This event is a potluck. Sharing food and drink is welcome but not required. Please make sure you label your item for folks with allergies. Adult beverages may be served to attendees over 21.

* **Level:** Everyone! This is a social event where there might be shop talk, but no lessons.
* **Timing:** 2 hours
* **Location:** Coworking/Textiles suite 206
* **Prerequisites:** Knitting, crocheting, or other hand work basics are prerequisites. This workshop provides mentorship on attendees’ existing skill set rather than teaching a new skill.
* **Certification:** No certifications are provided at this event
* **Age:** 14+

![A pull skein of purple yarn being wound on a ball winder from a hank draped over a wooden swift.](https://www.acemakerspace.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/textiles-swift-winder-168x300.jpg)Use of our swift and ball winder available to attendees upon request.

New Guests must [sign up for a FREE Guest Account and sign their liability waiver](https://www.acemakerspace.org/product/guest-account/) before this event starts. Add the FREE account on the Ace makerspace website check out or sign up here → [FREE Guest Account](https://www.acemakerspace.org/product/guest-account/)

Ace Makerspace is a 501(c)(3) non-­profit member-supported organization. All proceeds support Ace Makerspace and help us serve over 1200 people a year with innovative programs, equitable access to makerspace resources, and more creative, self-reliant communities in Oakland.

title: Monthly Cribbage Meetup!
description: Pop in when you can for a fun evening of cribbage with SF Card Players! Whether you're a beginner or seasoned pro, this event is perfect for anyone looking to enjoy a night of socializing and friendly
fullDescription:
Pop in when you can for a fun evening of cribbage with SF Card Players! Whether you're a beginner or seasoned pro, this event is perfect for anyone looking to enjoy a night of socializing and friendly competition. Come alone or bring a friend – all are welcome.

We have plenty of playing cards and cribbage boards but you're welcome to bring your own if you like. These are the house rules we'll play with which most cribbage apps follow: https://bicyclecards.com/how-to-play/cribbage

title: Build Enterprise Worthy LLM Inference with Open Source and Kubernetes
description:
Scaling LLMs to production introduces critical challenges: How do you orchestrate multi-node execution?

Optimize GPU scheduling? Achieve low-latency data transfers between nodes? This session reveals
fullDescription:
Scaling LLMs to production introduces critical challenges: How do you orchestrate multi-node execution?

Optimize GPU scheduling? Achieve low-latency data transfers between nodes? This session reveals how NVIDIA Dynamo and Azure Kubernetes Service solve these challenges together.

Through a real-world e-commerce recommendation scenario, discover how open-source frameworks and managed Kubernetes unlock enterprise-grade inference performance.

Learn to harness advanced hardware like GB200 NVL72, optimize distributed workloads on AKS, and deliver cost-efficient AI at scale to turn infrastructure complexity into competitive advantage.

title: Crochet Club (All Fiber Artists Welcome!)
description: Join us for our weekly fiber artists meetup every Tuesday night 6-9pm at Sana’a Cafe in Temescal on Telegraph! All fiber artists and skill levels are welcome! It’s free to join and I have community su
fullDescription:
Join us for our weekly fiber artists meetup every Tuesday night 6-9pm at Sana’a Cafe in Temescal on Telegraph! All fiber artists and skill levels are welcome! It’s free to join and I have community supplies if you don’t have your own!

The whole cafe is reserved for us and usually about 75 fiber artists attend each week. In addition to our Meetup group, I also post about all of the events I host on my IG @jennyandcabbage 🥰

fullDescription:
Join us Tues night for some Mandarin conversation, mahjong, pizza and beers!

**Min. RSVP Requirement:** 4 people

Whether you're a beginner or fluent speaker, this event is designed to foster a fun and inclusive environment for practicing Mandarin and connecting with fellow language enthusiasts. Don't miss out on this opportunity to expand your language skills while enjoying great company and a pint.

See you there!

We politely ask that everyone try to speak to the full extent of their own level to make the most out of the experience (it's ok if you aren't fluent!)

title: San Francisco AI & ML Professionals, Investors and Start-Ups Networking
description:
🔔 **Please Note:** RSVP on Meetup **does not guarantee entry**.
To attend, you **must register through the official ticket link** here: [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1984710878665?aff=oddtdtcreator](
fullDescription:
🔔 **Please Note:** RSVP on Meetup **does not guarantee entry**.
To attend, you **must register through the official ticket link** here: [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1984710878665?aff=oddtdtcreator](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1984710878665?aff=oddtdtcreator)

Step into the vibrant world of artificial intelligence and machine learning at our exclusive San Francisco AI & Machine Learnig Network Mixer. This event caters to innovators, thinkers, and investors in the AI and ML spheres, providing a unique opportunity to connect with industry leaders, discover cutting-edge projects, and explore potential collaborations.

**Event Highlights:**
**One-Minute Presentations:** A rapid-fire session where AI and ML startups can pitch their innovations and business ideas directly to a curated audience of peers and potential investors. Each presentation is limited to one minute, emphasizing clarity and impact.
**Structured Networking Opportunities:** Engage in meaningful discussions with fellow innovators and industry leaders. This event is designed to foster connections that lead to collaboration, investment, and growth.
**Investment & Business Growth Opportunities** – Discover potential deals, secure funding, and connect with professionals offering valuable expertise.

**Who Should Attend:**

* **Emerging AI and ML Startups:** Showcase your innovations and seek partnerships or funding in a focused setting.
* **Investors:** Find pioneering startups within the AI and ML domains that align with your investment criteria.
* **AI and ML Professionals:** Network with peers, discuss advancements and industry challenges, and potentially find your next career move.
* **Students and Academics:** Immerse yourself in the industry environment, gain insights, and connect with potential mentors and collaborators.

**Why This Event is a Must:**

* **Effective Networking:** Tailored specifically for the AI and ML communities, this mixer facilitates targeted networking, helping you build meaningful connections that can lead to collaborative projects or ventures.
* **Opportunity for Exposure:** Startups can gain visibility by presenting their projects during one-minute pitches, directly communicating their value to an audience that includes potential investors and collaborators.
* **Insight into Industry Trends:** Keep up-to-date with the latest research and commercial opportunities in AI and ML through conversations with industry insiders and thought leaders.
* **Career Opportunities:** Professionals looking to advance in the AI and ML fields can discover new opportunities and expand their networks in a dynamic, supportive environment.

Don’t miss this opportunity to engage with the forefront of AI and ML innovation. Whether you’re looking to invest, collaborate, or just learn about the next big thing in tech, our San Francisco AI & ML Network Mixer is the place to be. Secure your spot today and be part of shaping the future of technology!

**Why attend:**
**1-Minute Elevator Pitches:** Every attendee will have the chance to do a **short, 1-minute pitch** to the room — introduce yourself, share what you’re building or working on, and let others know what kind of co-founder, partner, or expertise you’re looking for.
**Networking**: Experience an evening of dynamic networking in a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere. Meet IT professionals from diverse backgrounds, including software development, cybersecurity, data science, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and more.
**Career Advancement Opportunities**: Explore potential career opportunities by interacting with representatives from leading tech companies and startups. Discover the latest job openings and internships in the IT and Technology sectors.

The event will be held in a sophisticated and vibrant venue, adorned with modern decor and equipped with state-of-the-art facilities to ensure your comfort and convenience throughout the day.

**Register now - space is limited to 50 attendees only!**
**P.S. Click Follow for future event alerts!**
group: San Francisco Startup Pitch and Networking

title: Passignano sul Trasimeno Walking Tour – The Golden Hour on Lake Trasimeno
description:
**REGISTRATION REQUIRED!** **[Click here to register](https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/9417747200822/WN_I_Y1JJ7-R82Ejk0lyO_lmA)**

Join us for a relaxing **virtual walking tour of Passignano s
fullDescription:
**REGISTRATION REQUIRED!** **[Click here to register](https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/9417747200822/WN_I_Y1JJ7-R82Ejk0lyO_lmA)**

Join us for a relaxing **virtual walking tour of Passignano sul Trasimeno**, a picturesque village overlooking the tranquil waters of Lake Trasimeno in the heart of Umbria.
During this immersive experience, we’ll wander through the **historic center**, where narrow streets, stone houses, and small squares reflect centuries of history and authentic Italian charm. Perched between gentle hills and the wide lake, Passignano offers some of the most beautiful views in central Italy.
As we explore the village, you’ll discover the peaceful rhythm of life by the lake, the medieval character of the old town, and the scenic waterfront that has made this destination a beloved retreat for travelers and locals alike.
With its relaxed atmosphere, lakeside beauty, and timeless character, Passignano sul Trasimeno perfectly captures the quiet charm of Umbria.
So grab a glass of Italian wine, sit back, and join us for a **beautiful sunset walk along Lake Trasimeno — all from the comfort of your home.**

**HOW TO JOIN:**
The tour will be hosted on Zoom. To register, you are **required to enter your full name** (first and last name).After the registration, you will receive via email a personal link to access the virtual event. To protect your privacy, your camera will be disabled during the event.
The tour will last approximately 1 hour. At the end of the tour you will be given the chance to interact and make questions just like a live tour. The tour takes place in English!
**PRICE**: **How much does the virtual tour cost?**
Our virtual tours are **tip-based** and not free, they rely entirely on contributions from participants. This means we do not impose a fixed ticket price to join. Every participant is welcome to take part in the tour and decide how much to contribute based on their satisfaction and personal budget. **You can leave a tip here:** [https://nextstop-italy.com/donation/](https://nextstop-italy.com/donation/)
❗**Please note**❗: To ensure the sustainability of the project, we regularly monitor the donations received, based on participation in previous tours, and, if necessary, we may apply participation limits or suspend accounts.This is essential to maintain the quality of our tours, ensure fairness, and continue rewarding the valuable work of our guides. *If you register for a tour but cannot attend live or watch the recording, we kindly ask you to unsubscribe before the tour begins. Otherwise, your registration will still be counted as participation.*

**Can’t watch our virtual tour live** due to the time zone or are you unable to join us live? Register at the virtual tour and we will send you the **recording** hence you can watch it in your own time! **Recordings are also tip-based**, as they support our cultural project and the work of our guides.
**Check out all our [Italy Virtual Tours](https://nextstop-italy.com/virtual-tours/)**
group: Italy Virtual Tours: Art, Culture and Beauty - San Francisco

description:

**LOCATION:**
**Sutter Station Tavern**
**554 Market St**
**San Francisco, CA 94104**
(Please note that the venue can be also accessed at **25 Sutter St**!)

Once arr
fullDescription:
Hello SF Social Language Lovers!

**LOCATION:**
**Sutter Station Tavern**
**554 Market St**
**San Francisco, CA 94104**
(Please note that the venue can be also accessed at **25 Sutter St**!)

Once arrived grab one of the name tags from the Host and write down your Name and the Languages that you: (want to) speak, are learning, are interested to learn or...all of the above!

Then find fellow language-exchange partners and/or share your native language and culture with others.

• ONE DRINK MINIMUM - This is a free group, which is great, but we do have to keep our venues welcoming us back. This means your minimum contribution is to buy yourself one drink or appetizer.

• Also, please do not forget to leave a TIP for the bartenders (typically a couple $$ per drink).

• Please remember that the bar is a public place and you're responsible for your personal possessions.

• ***The Goal of this Meetup is to help people practice languages and make friends in a comfortable environment. If something or someone makes you feel uncomfortable please do tell the Host.***

*Most importantly: have fun!*

We look forward to seeing you all soon!

Giovanni

PS: this Meetup is scheduled for every 3rd Wednesday of the month so...save the date!

**DISCLAIMER: By participating in this social event, you acknowledge and agree that the event Host/Organizer is not responsible for any injuries, damages, or economic losses that may occur. You fully accept all risks associated with your participation and hereby release the Host/Organizer from any and all liability.**

title: THE VETTED SOCIAL - Where every man comes highly recommended!
description:
We’re flipping the script. Welcome to **The Vetted Social**, an exclusive mixer for 30s/40s where the guest list is curated by the people who know best: **us.**

**[DISCOUNT TICKETS](https://www.event
fullDescription:
We’re flipping the script. Welcome to **The Vetted Social**, an exclusive mixer for 30s/40s where the guest list is curated by the people who know best: **us.**

**[DISCOUNT TICKETS](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/the-vetted-social-for-30s40s-tickets-1985076649696?aff=oddtdtcreator&keep_tld=true)**: $45/Early Birds/ $55/Door, if space permits. We have limited space. These events often sell out early, so we advise purchasing a ticket online as soon as possible.

**How It Works**
The concept is simple but effective: **No man enters alone.** To attend, every woman must bring one (and only one) single man as her "+1." This isn’t just any guy—he’s someone you can personally vouch for. He’s the "great guy" in your friend group, the hidden gem colleague, or the ex who is actually a wonderful human but just wasn’t *your* human.

DATE: April 15

TIME: 7:00 PM - 9:00 PM

PLACE: DACHA, [1085 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94109](https://www.google.com/maps/search/1085%0D%0A++++++++++++++Sutter+St,+San+Francisco,+CA+94109?entry=gmail&source=g)

**The Ground Rules**

* **The Entry Fee:** Your ticket is your +1. Women cannot enter without their vetted male counterpart, and men cannot enter without their female sponsor.
* **The Quality Control:** Ladies, by bringing him, you’re putting your "Stamp of Approval" on his character. You’re telling the room, *"He’s a good one."*
* **The Intent:** This is a high-trust environment designed to bypass the "stranger danger" of the apps. We’re crowdsourcing the vetting process so you can focus on the chemistry.

CO-SPONSORED by *The Society of Single Professionals, Singles Bay Area*, *BayAreaFun*, and dozens of singles organizations and meetups. All events benefit [The Seva Foundation](https://www.seva.org/), to restore sight to the blind.

fullDescription:
On the heels of the amazingly successful [Beer and Beats Meetup](https://www.meetup.com/anthemmusicacademy/events/313027749/?eventOrigin=group_events_list) that we run every week, we're now running an Ableton / DAW focused music-sharing/hangout/support group for all Ableton lovers out there. Don't worry: everyone at all levels of experience is welcome to join. The main goal of our meetup is to check out your DAW projects and geek out on production techniques. If you want to learn or share your knowledge, this is the place for you!

The event is free and you're welcome to BYOB.

Join our discord for info, no need to RSVP here on Meetup: https://discord.gg/UNgGtXmu6K

**[DISCOUNT TICKETS](https://www.event
fullDescription:
We’re flipping the script. Welcome to **The Vetted Social**, an exclusive mixer for 30s/40s where the guest list is curated by the people who know best: **us.**

**[DISCOUNT TICKETS](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/the-vetted-social-for-30s40s-tickets-1985076649696?aff=oddtdtcreator&keep_tld=true)**: $45/Early Birds/ $55/Door, if space permits. We have limited space. These events often sell out early, so we advise purchasing a ticket online as soon as possible. RSVP does NOT guarantee you a spot, only if you prepay by credit card.

**How It Works**
The concept is simple but effective: **No man enters alone.** To attend, every woman must bring one (and only one) single man as her "+1." This isn’t just any guy—he’s someone you can personally vouch for. He’s the "great guy" in your friend group, the hidden gem colleague, or the ex who is actually a wonderful human but just wasn’t *your* human.

DATE: April 15

TIME: 7:00 PM - 9:00 PM

PLACE: DACHA, [1085 Sutter St, San Francisco, CA 94109](https://www.google.com/maps/search/1085%0D%0A++++++++++++++Sutter+St,+San+Francisco,+CA+94109?entry=gmail&source=g)

**The Ground Rules**

* **The Entry Fee:** Your ticket is your +1. Women cannot enter without their vetted male counterpart, and men cannot enter without their female sponsor.
* **The Quality Control:** Ladies, by bringing him, you’re putting your "Stamp of Approval" on his character. You’re telling the room, *"He’s a good one."*
* **The Intent:** This is a high-trust environment designed to bypass the "stranger danger" of the apps. We’re crowdsourcing the vetting process so you can focus on the chemistry.

title: Come Sing Show Tunes!
description: This event consists of an informal concert in which the performers are all of us! We'll take turns singing Broadway show tunes. All musical levels are welcome, whether you're an aspiring musical theat
fullDescription:
This event consists of an informal concert in which the performers are all of us! We'll take turns singing Broadway show tunes. All musical levels are welcome, whether you're an aspiring musical theater star or just someone who belts out show tunes in the shower.

If you feel shy about singing a solo, it's okay to come and listen. We'll also do some group singing.

Please think ahead and pick one or two solos you'd enjoy singing (this is not a firm commitment, you can change your mind if you get the jitters). Depending on how many people choose to sing, you'll either get to sing one or both of your songs.

We don't always have an accompanist, so after you pick your songs, please search for suitable accompaniments on YouTube (you can search for "Songname karaoke" or "Songname accompaniment"). Once you've found accompaniments you like, please add links for your accompaniments to this spreadsheet so we can have them ready to play at the gathering:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ftn_81hdqtmYb8_QlKhpyzlu4FwKDDRZGZ3YT2x8qc8/edit?gid=0#gid=0

In the second hour of the gathering we'll schmooze and do more informal singing, and eat snacks -- (optional) feel free to bring a snack to share.

For covid safety, please don't attend if you or a household member have ANY cold or flu symptoms, or if you were recently exposed to covid or flu. We will have open windows and air purifiers running.
group: Berkeley Broadway Musicals Singing Group (In Person)

title: Pack Like a Pro: Travel Tips for Women 2026
description:
***This is a one-hour online class hosted by [Bold Spirit Travel LLC](https://www.boldspirittravel.com/newsletter).***

*\*Get a ticket to this class here: [Eventbrite](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/19
fullDescription:
***This is a one-hour online class hosted by [Bold Spirit Travel LLC](https://www.boldspirittravel.com/newsletter).***

*\*Get a ticket to this class here: [Eventbrite](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1983656451846?aff=oddtdtcreator)*

Unsure what to bring with you when you travel?

Have you been the woman at the airline check-in counter **frantically taking "essentials" out of her luggage** because your bag weight is *waaaay* over?

Or, worse yet, have you had to **pay more** to check an overweight bag? (Yikes.)

Ladies, many of us could use some help when it comes to packing. Join us for this online session to learn to be a better traveler.

**During this class you will learn:**

1. Tips for picking out the right suitcase and backpack
2. Ideas on how to create a capsule wardrobe for your trip
3. What you really do need to bring with you on each trip
4. About shoe choice
5. How to wash clothes on the road
6. Necessary gadgets (and the ones we can live without)
7. Mistakes we've made
8. How to leave room for fun shopping
9. To cube or not to cube?
10. What you need on the flight
11. And a whole lot more!

At the end of this session, we will provide you with a list of packing tips to make your next trip that much easier (use it as a checklist and stress less).

**There will be Q&A.**

* You can ask questions at the end of the talk.
* Come with your own tips, too, to share with other women.
* Is there a bag you love? A great travel shoe? A clothing brand that never wrinkles? Come tell us about what works for you and also learn from us and the audience.
* Use our tried and true tips to get ready for your next adventure.

**ZOOM LINK**

[https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85292245425](https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85292245425)

**FAQ**

**How do I get the link?**
You'll get it via email the day of the event. The Zoom link is also posted above.
*\*Get a ticket to this class here: [Eventbrite](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1983656451846?aff=oddtdtcreator)*

**What if I want to learn more about Bold Spirit Travel?**

* Then you for sure want to be on our newsletter list here: [https://www.boldspirittravel.com/newsletter](https://www.boldspirittravel.com/newsletter)

**I can't get into the event. Can someone help?**

* [The best thing to do is email us or text if the event has already started.](https://www.boldspirittravel.com/contact)

**[Wait..you](http://Wait..you) do trips for solo women travelers?**

* Yep. You can see all the trips we do here: [TRIPS FOR WOMEN](https://www.boldspirittravel.com/journeys)

title: The Future of Developer Experience: Documentation, AI, and APIs
description: Write the Docs Bay Area is bringing together the community for an evening focused on the future of developer experience. Modern developer platforms rely on high-quality documentation to help users und
fullDescription:
Write the Docs Bay Area is bringing together the community for an evening focused on the future of developer experience. Modern developer platforms rely on high-quality documentation to help users understand APIs, build faster, and solve problems efficiently. As AI tools reshape workflows, documentation teams are rethinking how content is created, maintained, and delivered.
Join technical writers, developer advocates, engineers, and product leaders for an evening of insights, discussion, and meaningful connections.

**Why Attend? (From Our Last Event)**

Here’s what attendees said about our March 19 event:

* "The panelists were excellent."
* "Great questions and answers from the panel."
* "Very knowledgeable speakers."
* "Loved the candor and willingness to share."
* "In-person discussions made it even better."

Event Impact (03/19/26):

* 90.9% found the content relevant
* 81.8% were returning attendees
* 90.9% made a professional connection

**Event Agenda**
6:00 – 6:30 PM
Check-in and networking (pizza and beverages sponsored by Falconer)
6:30 – 6:45 PM
Community announcements
6:45 – 7:00 PM
Opening remarks + Technical Writing Trends (March–April 2026)
7:00 – 8:00 PM
Talks
8:00 – 8:30 PM

Networking and wrap-up
Hard stop at 8:30 PM.

Optional post-event networking location will be announced during the event.

**Speakers**
Carly Vanderwert
Senior Manager, Developer Education at Twilio
Talk title: TBD

Speaker #2: TBD

Speaker #3: TBD

**Venue Capacity**
Limited to 80 attendees

**Security Requirements**

* Valid photo ID required for entry
* Check-in required upon arrival
* Entry is not guaranteed after 7:00 PM (no re-entry after this time)

**RSVP**
Spots are limited.
Once the combined RSVP count across Luma and Meetup reaches 80, waitlists will open on both platforms.

👉 RSVP early to secure your spot.

**Community Guidelines**

This event is hosted by Write the Docs Bay Area and follows the Write the Docs Code of Conduct.
Please help us be respectful of the venue by leaving the space as we found it. This helps us continue hosting events at great locations like this one 🙂.

**Tips on planning your commute to the meetup.**
📍 Venue Address: 101 Spear St (5th Floor)
Arrival Time: 6:00 PM \| Location: Spear & Mission St\, SF

Public Transportation (Best Option)

Avoid rush hour traffic by using Embarcadero Station, just a 5-minute walk away.
\- BART:
\- Muni Metro: J\, K\, L\, M\, N\, or T lines all stop at Embarcadero\.
\- Walking Directions: Exit the station onto Market St\, walk one block south\, and turn right onto Spear St\.

**Parking & Driving**

Expect heavy bridge and downtown traffic. We recommend these specific lots:

\- Board an SF\-bound BART train that stops at Embarcadero Station \(lines include Richmond→Daly City or Pittsburg/Bay Point→SFO/Millbrae\)\. Trains run every 10–15 minutes around 6 PM; travel time depends on your starting point \(10–40 min\)\.
\- 110 Main St Lot: \(5\-min walk\) Best for late stays\. Open 24/7\.
\- Embarcadero Center 4 Garage: \(7\-min walk\) Large\, reliable\, and open 24/7\.
\- Street Parking: Meters in this zone generally become free at 6:00 PM\, but spots are extremely rare\. Check signs carefully for "Tow\-Away" or "Commercial Only" restrictions\.
\- ⚠️ Avoid: The Rincon Center Garage at 121 Spear\, as it typically closes right at 6:00 PM\.

**Pro Tips**

\- Pre\-book Parking: Use SpotHero or ParkMobile for the 110 Main St lot to secure a spot and a lower rate\.
\- Vehicle Safety: Do not leave any bags or items visible inside your car \(even in "secure" garages\)\.

fullDescription:

Join us for an evening filled with insightful discussions on the latest in Data Engineering at the SF Bay Area Data Engineering Happy Hour. Whether you’re a seasoned data engineer or just starting out in the field, this event offers a valuable opportunity to hear fellow data engineers share their learnings, network with like-minded professionals, and expand your knowledge base.

title: Astrophotography 101: Intro to Deep Sky Imaging
description: Want to learn how to take pictures of Deep Sky Objects in the night sky? Ever wanted to learn how the amazing images from Hubble and James Webb are made? Whether you have specific questions about your
fullDescription:
Want to learn how to take pictures of Deep Sky Objects in the night sky? Ever wanted to learn how the amazing images from Hubble and James Webb are made? Whether you have specific questions about your own astrophotography process, or you are new to night photography, this workshop will provide one-on-one time to help you in your journey of night sky photography.

In this workshop we will focus specifically on deep sky astrophotography and take you through the full process: from operating an astrophotography ready telescope rig to processing a finished photo. We will show you how this type of astrophotography doesn’t have to involve investing thousands of dollars into astronomy gear and will still provide you with insight of the night sky and how to navigate it.

You will be trained on how to operate astronomy gear that you can rent through the [East Bay Astronomical Society’s](https://eastbayastro.org/) Loaner Scope Program and gain introductory knowledge on how to get started capturing images of the night sky. Feel free to bring your own equipment or images to process.
This workshop is part of a 3-part series of introductory astrophotography 101 workshops that can be taken together or independently. Workshops will have a combination of classroom time and time outside taking pictures of the night sky. All equipment and laptops are provided.

Note - this is a ticketed, paid workshop. Cost is $75, or $60 for Chabot members. Please purchase a ticket and pay through [Chabot's website.](https://chabotspace.org/calendar/astrophotography-101-intro-to-deep-sky-imaging/)
group: CDARC - California Dark Sky Astronomy Research Community

description: **First Date Co.** is where Bay Area single professionals meet, **off the apps and in real life.** Click here to join: [Bay Area Professionals Singles Mixer | Wine Social (40s& 50s)](https://luma.com/
fullDescription:
**First Date Co.** is where Bay Area single professionals meet, **off the apps and in real life.** Click here to join: [Bay Area Professionals Singles Mixer | Wine Social (40s& 50s)](https://luma.com/d6c84bmu)

We host curated, high-vibe events for professionals who are busy yet want to add fun, love and glitter to their life. They're ambitious even with their dating goals, and **done wasting time on swipes that go nowhere**. ​
Our guests are **professionals, creatives, founders, and go-getters, busy, social, and smart** enough to know that love deserves better than another dating app. ​
This isn’t just speed dating, this is **First Date energy, on your terms.**
**How it Works:**
You’ll have the chance to meet **everyone attending** through a series of short, relaxed conversations.
⏱️ Each conversation lasts **about 3–5 minutes**, just enough time to see if there’s a spark.
💬 Every table will have **fun icebreaker cards** to help keep the conversation flowing naturally.
🤝 Our organizer will guide the rotations and be there throughout the evening to make sure **everyone feels comfortable, welcomed, and enjoys the experience.**
After the rounds, you’ll have time to relax, mingle, and reconnect with anyone you felt a spark with, or **share their name with us, and we will let them know a day after, that you'd want to pick up where you left off! Yay!**
​
🌸🍷One glass of wine included. ​
🩷/💙 Events are always 50/50 ratio men/women. ​
🎟️ Events are intimate in a romantic setting, no poud bars.
🤵‍♂️👗Dress to impress, think something between cocktail & business casual ✨
🔗 LinkedIn is required.
💬 Come for the vibes, stay for the conversations. ​
💸 If you're here you can afford it, and you're worth it.
Rest Assured:
​✅ You’re *only* charged after your registration is reviewed and approved.
❌ Tickets are non-refundable, as we reserve your place with the venue and pay them in advance.
✅ If we cancel the event due to any reason such as 50/50 ratio not met, we'll automatically fully refund you. ​
***Note:*** *Please make sure you fall within the event’s age range before booking. To keep the experience fair for all guests, we will decline entry if this requirement isn’t met. Tickets in this case are non-refundable.*
**Let’s get you out of the chat and into the moment.**
Click here to join: [Bay Area Professionals Singles Mixer | Wine Social (40s& 50s)](https://luma.com/d6c84bmu)

title: Enterprise AI Panel
venue: 101 Howard St, University of San Francisco - Downtown Campus, San Francisco, CA 94105
description: University of San Francisco Data Institute, MS in Data Science and AI, School of Management, and The Center for Asia and Pacific are presenting you an Enterprise AI Panel on Aril 17, 2-4pm, at the USF
fullDescription:
University of San Francisco Data Institute, MS in Data Science and AI, School of Management, and The Center for Asia and Pacific are presenting you an Enterprise AI Panel on Aril 17, 2-4pm, at the USF Downtown Campus (101 Howard Street, San Francisco).

Enterprises are rapidly adopting AI to transform how they operate, make decisions, and deliver value. This panel brings together practitioners working with AI inside large organizations to share what’s really happening beyond the headlines: how companies are using AI today across functions like operations, marketing, product, and customer service, and what’s coming next as tools and capabilities evolve. You’ll also learn what skills employers are looking for, how AI is reshaping early-career paths, and practical ways to start building AI literacy now.

Register here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScvavKFxf2euRiFK5s5mZ32LXe-v2Xr8Pj5L3zKbTzuhfXIaA/viewform

title: Superprime Steakhouse (Japanese Influenced)
description:
Chef Marc Zimmerman [SUPERPRIME](https://www.superprimesf.com/)
Formerly Yokai a sleek, high-end Japanese listening bar and charcoal grill known for serving premium Japanese whisky, and binchotan-gril
fullDescription:
Chef Marc Zimmerman [SUPERPRIME](https://www.superprimesf.com/)
Formerly Yokai a sleek, high-end Japanese listening bar and charcoal grill known for serving premium Japanese whisky, and binchotan-grilled skewers.
Superprime embraces the same aesthestic and features a few of Yokai's menu favorites, but is uniquely its own.

Courtesy of their webpage: "An insatiable passion for the best beef on the planet. Crafted by Chef Marc Zimmerman, sonically elevated by vintage JBL audio, and accompanied by a robust beverage program featuring Japanese whisky and boutique wine and spirits. "

Superprime is truly focused on beef with an extensive menu featuring Japanese A5 Wagyu; Pictured is Full Blood Austrialian. Not into beef? Select from Chilled and Raw, Poached Halibut, or grilled Duck Breast . Perhaps savory side items such as fried wild rice or roasted beets will satiate your palate?

**Adminstrative Trivia**
Please expect to pay at least $80 per person,(Wagyu will be much more per 3oz increments). Beverage will increase this amount. Each diner is responsible for the total cost of what they order. Take raw bill x1.36 for your total payment (9% tax, 20% tip, 7% SF mandate). Organizer will manage **one singular bill and form of payment** for the check. Please be prepared to **pay the organizer by Cash, Venmo, AppleCash.**

Restaurant Located at 545 Mission St @ Shaw Alley
Street parking on Mission is limited.
Garage parking 535 Mission St Lot#86 at 36 Minna.
Bus Muni 14, 14R, Golden Gate Transit 130, 101, and 150 Bus.

Dress is business Casual.
Open RSVP may be limited.
Organizer reserves right to manage accordingly.
