title: Santa Barbara County Highpoint (bike + hike, 40 miles/6k' gain, date open)
description: The highest point in Santa Barbara County (Big Pine Mtn) is only 6500', but it's a beast to get to: a round trip of about 40 miles & 6,000' of gain. However, it's almost all fire road and most people
fullDescription:
The highest point in Santa Barbara County (Big Pine Mtn) is only 6500', but it's a beast to get to: a round trip of about 40 miles & 6,000' of gain. However, it's almost all fire road and most people bike as much of it as possible and hike the rest. We'll do this in one long day.

See past trip reports here: http://cohp.org/ca/california.xml#Santa%20Barbara

It looks like he biked the whole thing:
https://www.strava.com/activities/11334238741

The good news is there don't appear to be many steep ascents; I assume it's like the San Luis Obispo highpoint which I biked/hiked:
https://goalhiking.org/g/biking-caliente-mountain

The date is open because we need to do this between when the rains stop and the weather gets too hot for most people. The bonus is that might coincide with a wildflower bloom in the very attractive Carrizo Plain that's nearby.

We'll do this at a moderate pace and we might finish at night. We'll all stick together, no tigers. And, I'm ruling out eBikes since I want all of us to earn this. However, I had a lower gear range installed on my (clunky, 20+ year old) mountain bike so you might want to consider that. Barely moving is better than pushing.

Everyone gets added to the waitlist, and to get off it you have to leave a comment or send me a DM. I'll post more details as we get closer to Spring.

(Image via peakbagger)
group: " Last-Minute Backpackers " Camping, Hiking, Outdoors & More

title: Celebrate Earth Day 2026!
description: Come celebrate Earth Day 2026 at Middle Harbor Shoreline Park! Bring friends, family, co-workers, classmates, comrades to help protect our beautiful San Francisco Bay, support wildlife, & enhance your
fullDescription:
Come celebrate Earth Day 2026 at Middle Harbor Shoreline Park! Bring friends, family, co-workers, classmates, comrades to help protect our beautiful San Francisco Bay, support wildlife, & enhance your health! come clean the shore and implement community-scale solutions to tackle climate change. This is your invitation to take direct action!

There will be morning refreshments and lunch\* generously provided by the Port of Oakland.

Schedule:

9:00am \~ Checkin, Sign Waivers\*\* & Enjoy Morning Snacks

9:15am \~ Welcome & Safety Instructions

9:30am - 11:30AM \~ Beach Cleanup & Educational Activities

11:30am \~ Group Appreciations (Most Unusual Items), Photo & Prizes

Noon \~ Lunch (provided by the Port of Oakland\*)

\*RSVP by Friday, April 10th so we are sure to have enough food & supplies.
Include the NUMBER # of people in your group & # Vegetarians (as relevant)

\*\*Everyone is required to sign a waiver form prior to participating in the cleanup, when you arrive at the checkin table.

\*\*Youth (under the age of 18) must be accompanied by an adult. A parent or legal guardian of youth under the age of 18 must sign the youth waiver form. **Students may fulfill community service hours with this activity.**

\*\*\* Rain Cancels

This event is registered with the City of Oakland Earth Day Events.
https://www.oaklandca.gov/Community/Events-Culture/Events/Oakland-Earth-Day

**ACTIVITIES**
Meet neighbors and other Friends of Middle Harbor Shoreline Park. Collect and record trash found along the shore. Remove weeds and plant native salt marsh plants. Observe SF Bay wildlife such as migratory shorebirds with an expert birder.
Local community &/or science based organizations will be onsite to offer additional educational activities.

Prizes are awarded to children and adults (especially those who organize a group of 10+ to attend), and most unusual objects found and shared. Binoculars may be borrowed for birding (weather depending).

**WHAT TO BRING**
You are encouraged to bring your own gloves and buckets and a reusable water bottle to stay hydrated and reduce waste. Nurture NATURE, the Port of Oakland & the City of Oakland provide disposable nitrile gloves, plastic trash bags and buckets.

Remember to wear clothes that can get dirty, closed-toe shoes, an extra layer to keep you warm and a hat for sun/wind protection.

\*You're welcome to bring your own lunch to enjoy in the park after the cleanup. If you'd like to partake in the Port provided lunch, RSVP BY 4/10/26.

**ACCESSIBILITY, HEALTH & SAFETY**
Middle Harbor Shoreline Park has wheelchair accessible paths and bathrooms. A limited supply of pick-up sticks are reserved for people who cannot bend or grasp with their hands.

We ask that if you're not feeling well, have a temperature or have known exposure to Covid-19 that you stay home and rest. Masks are optional.

We provide nitrile gloves for your safety during the cleanup and anti-bacterial soap.

This is an outdoor event and a site with unique (bio)hazards, such as: rocky rip rap, hypodermic needles, broken glass, rusty nails, construction materials, changing tide levels. Please take care of your self and your guests!

**ALL ARE WELCOME, PLEASE RSVP**
Whether you come solo or as part of a large group, all are welcome!

You may alternately RSVP (esp. large groups) via this Nurture NATURE form: https://forms.gle/s2HrjUKtMwp77ieFA

**Stay in touch with our Naturalist Programs by connecting with Nurture NATURE on Instagram: [https://www.instagram.com/nurture___nature/](https://www.instagram.com/nurture___nature/)**
group: Friends of Middle Harbor Shoreline Park

title: Ceremony Volunteer Training Video Course
description:
**Hello Familia!**

***\*Immediate access, video course always available.***

If the Path of Sacrament has resonated with you, becoming a Pachamama Facilitator could be a journey that is waiting for y
fullDescription:
**Hello Familia!**

***\*Immediate access, video course always available.***

If the Path of Sacrament has resonated with you, becoming a Pachamama Facilitator could be a journey that is waiting for you. We understand not everyone is ready to become a Shaman (or not just yet). Therefore, we want to invite you to begin your path as a Ceremonial Retreat Volunteer. Work side by side with Manuel, Andres Yvonne, Laura or myself while you practice the art of Spiritual Service (Seva).

**Become a volunteer and assist in the Sacrament ceremonies in:**

* California
* Oregon
* Nevada
* Washington
* Texas

**Learn and understand the details of assisting a Shaman or Hampeq.**

Volunteers have the opportunity to keep working with the wisdom of the Andes and the teachings of sacred plant sacrament while continuing to synchronize with the PSP and with the path of our Tradition (Q'ero cosmovision) that we share.

A volunteer has both: responsibilities and additional benefits. These 2 will help develop a deeper Ayni (Balanced Reciprocity) and Karpay (Personal Power) for all parts of yourself.

Additionally, being exposed to a more consistent space of healing and growth is a fast-track Path to your realization without cutting any corners.

**BENEFITS OF VOLUNTEERING**

The 1st Benefit is of an Ayni Nature.

In exchange for your Seva (Service) you will be able to sit with the medicines In ceremony while you assist.

Depending on the Medicine Facilitator and the group, you can go lighter or deeper on your personal journey. Always keeping in mind that this Volunteer Role is one being in Service at all times.

**The 2nd Benefit is of personal growth nature**

Being of Service offers you much space for personal reflection: As you help others with their processes, you will have the opportunity to reflect upon your own inner work. Every person you come in contact with, is an opportunity for personal growth. Being alongside Manuel, Andres, Yvonne, Laura or Myself, allows you to introspect much more deeply into those areas of your life on continuous 1-on-1 with the wisdom.

**The 3rd Benefit is of leadership skills nature**

Taking responsibility for different processes, learning to follow specific instructions with humility, and integrating the ABCs of running a ceremony, prepares you to not only hold space for groups of brothers and sisters looking for adequate spaces of healing, but also begin to learn how leadership is exemplified at larger scales of profound transformation settings.

**Some of the things you will learn are:**

* Ceremonial Retreat Protocol
* Understanding Ceremony Poq'po
* Energy Movement: Grounding, Feather work, Incense work
* Accommodations
* Feedback Techniques
* Ceremony Responsibilities
* Resolving Chaos (Archetype work)
* Protection from Kalka-Kawsay (entities)
* Yananyin and Masintin Basics.

Please note that you must be either enrolled in an ongoing Shamanic initiation or planning to enroll on the upcoming Shamanic Initiation (Module 1). This is a pre-requisite.

If you are interested in more information, please click [HERE](https://api.leadconnectorhq.com/widget/form/pAuPN6klsaPGV96RsL3p)

We hope to meet you in the path.

**Munay!**

**Noccan Kani**
**Vianney Cruz**

***\*Immediate access, video course always available.***

**[WHATSAPP](https://walink.co/22384e)**
**[CONTACT US](https://api.leadconnectorhq.com/widget/form/pAuPN6klsaPGV96RsL3p)**

title: E-bike Tour San Francisco to Sausalito
description: This iconic bike tour begins in San Francisco’s Fisherman’s Wharf – one of the only true wharfs of its kind left in America! From there, your journey follows the curve of the coast to see sea lions, t
fullDescription:
This iconic bike tour begins in San Francisco’s Fisherman’s Wharf – one of the only true wharfs of its kind left in America! From there, your journey follows the curve of the coast to see sea lions, the military fort and the city’s best view of the bridge. You’ll bike over the Golden Gate Bridge for priceless views of the area’s unique topography and skyline. Once over the ”International Orange”, coast downhill into sunny Sausalito, where you’ll hop on a ferry with your bike in tow for an easy ride back to the wharf. Tour is 2-3 hours. There is also an option to remain in Sausalito for the afternoon, and the bike company will return your bike, and we can take the ferry back to the ferry building in the late afternoon. We will meet at 2661 Taylor Street, San Francisco at 9:45 am.

Tour will be run by experienced and highly rated tour guides. You will get equipped with a helmet and a properly-fitted e-bike. Each tour will start with a bike and riding safety demonstration. Feel the joy of riding, and use pedal assist as needed, you can choose your level of power. Want a workout, use less assistance, want to avoid breaking a sweat, go full assist!

If you are signed up for our Bike and Barge in Burgundy this June, this is the perfect opportunity to try out your gear (and even your bike shorts!), get familiar with the bikes, and meet your fellow traveler. All women are welcome to join, even if you are not signed up for a trip.

Note: You do not need previous e-bike experience to join these tours. You should be familiar with riding a bike, however, and if you don't have recent experience possibly practice a bit before joining us.

Sign Up Here:
[Wanderlust Women E-bike Trips](https://wanderlust-women.com/new-local-trips-1)
group: Wanderlust Women 50+ Active Hike & Bike Trips-Solo welcome!

description: **[ PLEASE NOTE: Due to our Meetup account limits, RSVP's are limited to 10 people. We've also listed the Exhibition on Eventbrite - Additional RSVP available here: [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/28th-
fullDescription:
**[ PLEASE NOTE: Due to our Meetup account limits, RSVP's are limited to 10 people. We've also listed the Exhibition on Eventbrite - Additional RSVP available here: [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/28th-annual-artesteem-exhibition-love-surrounds-us-tickets-1979943108135?aff=socials](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/28th-annual-artesteem-exhibition-love-surrounds-us-tickets-1979943108135?aff=socials) ]**

The 2026 Exhibition marks ArtEsteem's 28th year of celebrating and showcasing the critical artistic expression of our youth!

On Saturday, April 18th at Studio One Art Center (365 45th Street, Oakland, CA 94609) from 1:00 to 4:00pm, you can journey through the minds of students from Bay Area schools as they use fundamental, experimental, and sustainable art practices to explore the world around them.

The artwork will be available for sale, with 50% of the proceeds going directly to the student artist and the remaining 50% going toward next year's ArtEsteem programming.

Get ready to discuss the artwork with the students and their teaching artists, enjoy a stunning cultural arts performance, participate in fun outdoor art activities, and snack on tasty bites!

Check out last year's artwork here: [https://www.artesteem.org/exhibition-2025](https://www.artesteem.org/exhibition-2025)

Donate to support ArtEsteem programming here: [https://www.artesteem.org/donate](https://www.artesteem.org/donate)

title: Virtual Ukulele Rebellion 107 -- Celebrating Six Years on Zoom!
description: San Francisco Ukulele Rebellion met on Zoom for the first time on April 18, 2020. This April, we'll be celebrating our **sixth** anniversary on Zoom! As we did for our previous anniversaries, we'll pl
fullDescription:
San Francisco Ukulele Rebellion met on Zoom for the first time on April 18, 2020. This April, we'll be celebrating our **sixth** anniversary on Zoom! As we did for our previous anniversaries, we'll play **your favorite songs and/or songs that might have helped you get through the Covid-19 pandemic**. *Please consider sharing favorite songs that **you have NOT shared** in a **previous** San Francisco Ukulele Rebellion Zoom meetup*. We'll play music from 7:00 pm to 9:00 pm Pacific Daylight Time.

To address recent instances of Zoom-bombing, I have created a waiting room and I will be looking at and implementing additional security procedures for access to Zoom. Please make sure your Zoom name is one I recognize. Peoplw who appear in the Zoom waiting room will not be admitted to the Zoom meeting if any of the hosts do not recognize his or her name. The Zoom link will appear on this page when you RSVP. If Zoom asks for a passcode, the passcode is Rebellion. The passcode is case-sensitive.

If you'd like to lead a song, please let me know, either by posting here in the comments or by email, which song(s) you would like to lead. The cut-off time for requesting to lead a song is THE TUESDAY BEFORE THE MEETING (for this meeting, Tuesday April 14), even if you're going to be leading a song from one of the Daily Ukulele books. I'll post a songlist here in advance of our meeting.

IF YOU'D LIKE TO LEAD A SONG USING SHEET MUSIC OR A CHORD CHART THAT **ISN'T** IN THE YELLOW BOOK, THE BLUE BOOK, OR THE BBJ GROOVY SONGBOOK, EMAIL THE DOCUMENT TO ME IN DIGITAL FORMAT **IN ADVANCE** SO THAT I CAN UPLOAD IT TO DROPBOX AND POST IT ON THE SCREEN FOR OTHERS.

PLEASE NOTE: To accommodate all first song requests, we may not have time to get to all second (or third) song requests. When asking to lead more than one song, please indicate your first and second choices to ensure that, if we can't get to your second request, the song you'd most like to lead is included in that evening's song list. Thank you in advance for your willingness to lead songs and your understanding about how the song list for each meeting is prepared.
You can reach me by email at sfukerebellion@yahoo.com

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Song requests:

Tribute songs:
TBD [Dash Crofts]

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
I'll be sharing all song charts onscreen, but you can download digital copies of the Daily Ukulele books and the pdfs for tonight's songs by following links that will be posted in the comments.

The dropbox link to The Daily Ukulele.
[https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/uca82l70r9a4hgjqohsgq/Daily_ukulele_yellow_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=44gm13r5gbvhstiv7ibnuojt1&st=dvrcg7n4&dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/uca82l70r9a4hgjqohsgq/Daily_ukulele_yellow_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=44gm13r5gbvhstiv7ibnuojt1&st=dvrcg7n4&dl=0)

The dropbox link to the Daily Ukulele - Leap Year Edition:
[https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/hy9pnffip1lc1rxs5zc8q/Daily_Ukulele_blue_Leap_Year_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=oug9yq9ezckyly3ekj3su539u&st=2hgndbig&dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/hy9pnffip1lc1rxs5zc8q/Daily_Ukulele_blue_Leap_Year_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=oug9yq9ezckyly3ekj3su539u&st=2hgndbig&dl=0)

The Dropbox link to the Groovy Song Book used in the Bronco Billy Zoom jams:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/ezywmwbck8229gu/2021-05-22_v9_Official%20BBJ%20Virtual%20Song%20Packet.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/ezywmwbck8229gu/2021-05-22_v9_Official%20BBJ%20Virtual%20Song%20Packet.pdf?dl=0)

To participate, you will need to download the Zoom app to whatever device you'll be using (there are Apple, Android, and desktop (Mac and PC) versions). To be seen and heard, you will need a device with a built in camera and microphone or a device to which you have attached a webcam. title: Night Photography & Light Painting @ Palace of Fine Arts
description:
Let's do something different and learn light painting! It's a photography technique that uses a moving light source during a long-exposure photography to create striking visual effects.

We'll meet at
fullDescription:
Let's do something different and learn light painting! It's a photography technique that uses a moving light source during a long-exposure photography to create striking visual effects.

We'll meet at the Palace of Fine Arts at 8:00 PM. The location is subject to change if I find a better spot, but for now let's plan to meet there since it's known to be a great location for long-exposure night photography.

Please bring your own light source such as an LED wand, flashlight, pixel stick, glow stick, or small GRB light. A tripod is strongly recommended since without one the image can blur, the background may not stay sharp, and light trails can be harder to control.

Bringing a camera isn't required, though you may get better results with one than with a phone.

Disclaimer: I've never done light painting before, so this will be a learning experience with plenty of trial and error. Results may vary, but good vibes are guaranteed.

Lastly, this event is welcome to people of all ages and photography experience levels. Even if you're not interested in taking photos but want to hang out and grab drinks with us after, come through!

title: Mori Point Hike (Dogs Welcome)
description: In The Golden Gate National Parks, is Mori Point 110 acre reserve with the 32-acre wetland park, red-legged frog ponds, and a revived habitat for the San Francisco garter snake, once North America’s m
fullDescription:
In The Golden Gate National Parks, is Mori Point 110 acre reserve with the 32-acre wetland park, red-legged frog ponds, and a revived habitat for the San Francisco garter snake, once North America’s most endangered reptile. You will go through Hillsides with gnarled trees, fields of scrub, cliffs, dark sand beach, ocean views way up the coast, rocks, waves, and wildlife. And in the springtime you behold beautiful wildflower are in their peak, windy on the bluffs so dress accordingly. Stroll down to Laguna Salada and listen for the croaking of the red-legged frog. During WWII, workers used a pulley system of long cables attached to cement anchors to pull buckets of sand ashore. Several of these crumbling blocks remain on the western hillside. do a spot of whale watching or simply enjoy the waves of the Pacific Coast washing up onto the shores.

**Level:** Intermediate (several steep Hills)
**Total Distance:** 5.8 miles Semi-loop
**Hiking Time:** 2.5 - 3 hours
**Elevation:** 450 feet, total 900 feet
**Parking:** Free Parking
**Information:** Public Restrooms near Trailhead parking lot, but lots of Cafes and restaurants near the parking area, Restroom and Cafe at midway point, Dogs allowed on-leash on all trails

**GPS Coordinates for Trailhead:**
**Latitude:** 37\. 611265 \| **Longitude:** -122. 496926

**Meeting Point / Trailhead:** Pacifica Beach View Park 651 San Marlo Way Pacifica, CA 94044, I will parked near the trailhead.

**Map:** http://www.parksconservancy.org/assets/visit-your-parks/pdfs/mori-point-trail-map.pdf

**Hike Route: Starting point;** Along the beach trail at Rockway Beach Parking Lot, take the beach trail cross over a stream and climb up to the coastal bluffs, then go along the ridge and through a small canyon to a bluff, then take the high ridge trail all the way to Mori Point, **(short Break and group photo)** then, down old Mori point trail, then Left out on the beach Promenade straight to the end, Pacifica Pier **(Lunch Break cafe & restrooms)** then return back on the Promenade and Right up Old Mori point trail, then Right high Lower trail back to the bluff, then down through a small canyon return and head down Left stay on the trail left close to the Hill, then Right on the bike path and return to Rockway Beach Parking Lot...

**Directions:** From hwy 1, Right into Pacifica Pier, parallel to Santa Rosa Avenue, Pacifica

**Carpooling Link: (Sign up here)**
https://www.groupcarpool.com/t/cxa50v

**Carpooling Procedures:**
No carpool discussion in the comments, join the carpool link and contact the driver or rider directly.
**Thank you .......**

title: Curious Craft Club
description:
Congratulations! You’ve resolved to be more creative in 2026. You made a list of projects. You bought the supplies. This should be a happy moment.

And yet… five minutes in, you’re slumped in a pile o
fullDescription:
Congratulations! You’ve resolved to be more creative in 2026. You made a list of projects. You bought the supplies. This should be a happy moment.

And yet… five minutes in, you’re slumped in a pile of felt scraps and sequins. Everything you make looks bad or stupid. You’ve burned yourself on the hot glue gun again. You start wondering if you should pack it all up and take up a safer hobby, like birdwatching.

Before you give up—pause.

The answer is NO. You don’t need a new hobby. You don’t need another trip to Michaels. You just need to come make some Bad Art with us.

At Craft Curious Club, we believe bad art is good for the soul. For one afternoon, we invite you to put your inner critic to sleep and make mistakes on purpose. Mix clashing colors. Glob on the glitter. Draw with your non-dominant hand. Let happy accidents happen. You might surprise yourself.

Please join us for an afternoon of creative nonsense and low-stakes making. Arlene Shirlee will guide us through a series of pointless yet highly engaging activities designed to bring the fun back to creating.

This is a sliding fee scale of $15-$25!
Email events@stickyartlab.com for more information.

Snacks and light refreshments provided. You can expect to leave with 1–3 finished Bad Art masterpieces—perfect for your fridge, your desk, or as truly unforgettable white elephant gifts.

Come make something weird. We promise: it doesn’t have to be good. ✨
—Arlene Shirlee
Craft Curious Club

title: [Lunch] Quince - Reservation Confirmed, RSVP Closed
description:
**Quince**
*Chef Tusk’s approach to food is refined and contemporary, taking immense pride in offering guests a unique opportunity to taste exceptional ingredients at their purest.*

Michael and Linds
fullDescription:
**Quince**
*Chef Tusk’s approach to food is refined and contemporary, taking immense pride in offering guests a unique opportunity to taste exceptional ingredients at their purest.*

Michael and Lindsay Tusk curate a one of a kind experience, from decor, views, every step, every bite, every smell. An amazing afternoon is in store.

*Jackets are suggested, elegant dress is required, no shorts, flip flops.*

The five course meal will allow addons, a non-alcohoic pairing is $100. Their menu is the freshest ingredients and individual courses are not known at this time, there is a vegetarian and vegan option.

This is a pre-pay with refunds only if the venue cancels or I am unable to secure the reservation, and this is first pay first in, so I will keep track of the payment date and times.

***No Refunds after February 15, 2026. Event Closed after six people prepay or March 1, 2026.*** We will help you transfer your ticket to someone else if you need to cancel, and receive a refund that way, if that is possible.

I have it set for six people, I will get a smaller reservation (four) or less if we have less interest. I am sure myself and Myles will have a lovely time if only us two go, but we do hope for a full party.

Payment includes meal, addons for drinks, alcohol, additional courses will increase the cost. $308 includes tax, service charge and tip. Please send payment of $308 by Apple Cash or Zelle and put 'Quince' in the comments. Message me for payment information.

**History**
*Quince opened in 2003, and recently completed an eleven month renovation, changing their interior and dinner menu options.*

**Menus and Reviews**
[Quince](https://www.quincerestaurant.com)

**Administratriva**
Cost is $220, plus a service charge, 22% tip and tax. Total at time of booking is $308.

*Dress is eligent Jackets Preferred, no shorts, flip flops.*

**Taxes and Restaurant Fees**
Dining in San Francisco has a sales tax (8.625%) and probably an employee health mandate (3-10%), a tip (20%) may be automatically added, even on individual bills, if not we expect a 20% minimum tip to be paid for individual bills. We will try to get separate checks, but remember to bring cash.

Each diner is responsible for their own costs but we will have to consolidate and divvy up the final bill accordingly.

*Please bring cash or have Zelle or Apple Cash available.*

**Transportation to Quince**
MUNI: 8, 12, 30, T
Finding street parking in FIDI is usually difficult.
Parking garages can be found nearby

*Use event comments for Car Pooling and Ride Sharing.*

*Many thanks for the photos and history from restaurant website*

#EatSanFrancisco #SanFranciscoFoodies #SFFoodies

**Deposits and Prepays**
A deposit is applied to each attendee’s portion of the event (for example dinner) cost or returned to each attendee. Unexpected extra from individual deposits are returned to each attendee or left as tip.

A deposit is to defray hosts’ costs from no-shows, many restaurants charge for each guest missing.

Prepaid events should cover all event costs, except for extras which are noted in an event description. For example drinks and add ons are not usually included.

*Deposits and prepays are non-refundable.*

A host, at their desecration may have a cut off for refunds, usually one week to 72 hours before an event. Any refund may occur up to one week after an event, if there is a delay let your host know.

Under extreme circumstances (like a natural disaster) or if an event is cancelled by host or venue then deposits are returned. For large events please be patient, these refunds may take some time.

Some San Francisco Foodies events offer third party insurance and San Francisco Foodies cannot refund that cost.

Donations go directly to a non-profit and are ancillary to an event or events. San Francisco Foodies cannot refund these.

Third Party events collect funds directly from San Francisco Foodies members and San Francisco Foodies cannot issue a refund, but may help recover lost deposits or prepays on a case by case basis.

*San Francisco Foodies is willing and able to go to court to protect our community, hosts and members.*

title: The Nature of Creativity: Tapping in to the natural and synthetics dialogue
description:
Come join us for a deep dive into how different psychedelics, plant/fungi or synthetic, impact creativity.

You can also reserve your space at the Eventbrite link here: https://NatureOfCreativity.even
fullDescription:
Come join us for a deep dive into how different psychedelics, plant/fungi or synthetic, impact creativity.

You can also reserve your space at the Eventbrite link here: https://NatureOfCreativity.eventbrite.com

This 3-hour workshop starts out with a lecture/discussion highlighting reasons why people may choose one substance over the other for creative purposes. Then, after looking at art inspired by different kinds of psychedelics, we will make our own artwork exploring our thoughts and feelings about this complex issue. Throughout we will examine risks, benefits, and impact (positive and negative) of plant versus synthetic psychedelics.

This event is for beginners to psychonauts interested in using art and creative practices to explore a complex topic in the world of psychedelics.

**Absolutely no artistic experience or skill is necessary.** We will walk you through simple techniques that will make the art process not only approachable, easy, and fun, but something that deepens your understanding of yourself and the topic at hand.
***Please note that this workshop will not include the sharing or use of psychedelics. Art supplies will be included.***

**Facilitated by Larry Norris, Co-founder of ERIE: Entheogenic Research, Integration, and Education and Decriminalize Nature and Rebecca Wilkinson, Licensed Counselor and Art Therapist and Co-Founder of the Psychedelic Art Therapy Network. Larry and Rebecca are practicing artists and have experience with both natural and man-made medicines.**

\*Location next to Ashby Bart, plenty of parking available
group: ERIE: Entheogenic Research, Integration, and Education

Thanks for joining our wellness club event, it does take time and money to make it happen. Thank you for being prepared and supportive 🌿

Trails are part of nature and may not match everyone’s preferences. Dinner venues are selected based on group size, availability, and location. We do our best but can’t guarantee personal favorites.

**\*Participation in this event is strictly voluntary**! The event host/organizer assumes no responsibility for any injury, damage, or loss you may incur. You join and hike entirely at your own risk.

\*We are an intermediate hike group. Please be ready to keep up hiking with us from 5-8 miles without any health issues.

**\*LIABILITY WAIVER:** By signing up for the wellness walking/hiking event, you hereby release its organizers, hosts, and participants from any and all liability, claims, demands or causes of action that you now have, or may have in the future, for illness, injuries, damages, and/or economic loss of any kind or nature, arising in whole or in part out of the activities, advice, or information contained herein. You agree to full responsibility for your own health issues.

**\*DISCLAIMER:** By participating in this wellness hiking event, you understand that Meetup event Host/Organizer assumes ABSOLUTELY NO responsibility and you have to assume all at your own risk of injury, damage, and economic loss, without limitation, and release host/Organizer of all liability.

If anyone wishes to join our hike team, please answer the RSVP question every time for everyone's safety in the group. If you are unable to send me a message confirming that you are in good health and you are able to hike 5-8 miles without health issues we CANNOT approve you come to the hiking event.
\*Please don't just walk in. Thank you for your cooperation!

I'm looking forward to meeting, hiking with you and making good memories together.
Yours in good health! Dr. Heidi💝

title: 🍷 Secret Wine Bar | Blind Wine Society Night
description:
🍷 Secret Wine Bar \| Blind Wine Society Night

Palo Alto Blind Wine Society — Monthly Tradition

Some of the best wine experiences aren’t advertised.
You just have to know where to go.

Once a month,
fullDescription:
🍷 Secret Wine Bar \| Blind Wine Society Night

Palo Alto Blind Wine Society — Monthly Tradition

Some of the best wine experiences aren’t advertised.
You just have to know where to go.

Once a month, we step away from blind tasting grids and into something equally important: great wine, great people, and zero pressure.

This is our standing social night at the Secret Wine Bar — a hidden gem where the wines are always thoughtfully selected by Master Sommelier David Glancy, founder of the San Francisco Wine School.

Expect:
• Exceptional wines you might not normally encounter
• A relaxed, insider wine crowd
• Conversations that range from geeky to hilarious
• A chance to just enjoy wine without guessing it

No themes.
No exams.
No pressure.

Just great bottles and great company.

🍷 Why we go:
Because even serious tasters need nights where wine is simply fun again.

🍇 What makes this special:
David Glancy MS curates the wine list, which means the quality and interest level is consistently high. You never know exactly what will be featured, but it is always worth it.

📅 When: Last Saturday of every month
📍 Where: Secret Wine Bar
👥 Who: PABWS members and friends

💡 Good to know:
• Buy whatever wines interest you
• Stay as long or as short as you like
• New members welcome
• This is a social night, not a structured tasting

If you love discovering great wine with people who actually care about what’s in the glass, this is your kind of night.

Taste. Learn. Guess. Repeat.
(And sometimes… just drink.)

title: Sofreh: A Contemporary Approach to Classic Persian Cuisine
description:
For our next meetup, we’ll be cooking from *Sofreh* by Nasim Alikhani and Theresa Gambacorta.

We’ll be meeting at Jessica's home in the Inner Richmond, SF. Please bring at least one recipe’s worth of
fullDescription:
For our next meetup, we’ll be cooking from *Sofreh* by Nasim Alikhani and Theresa Gambacorta.

We’ll be meeting at Jessica's home in the Inner Richmond, SF. Please bring at least one recipe’s worth of food, prepared for 6 to 8 people.

**New policy: Please post your recipe in the comments before Wednesday, April 22, to keep your spot at the table. You'll be moved to "Not Going" if you don't post. You'll receive the address on Wednesday if you do post your dish.**

**As always, no shows and cancellations will get you removed from the group. Please let us know of any allergens in your dish beforehand and substitute if possible.**

--
The much-anticipated cookbook—an exquisite collection of Persian recipes—from the James Beard–nominated chef of Sofreh, one of Brooklyn’s most acclaimed restaurants.

Growing up in Isfahan, a province in central Iran, Nasim Alikhani was a passionate cook from childhood, spending the first years of her life in the kitchen alongside her mother. And so, when she departed after the revolution it was by re-creating the dishes of her youth that she was able to feel connected to her home. After decades of cooking for friends and family, at the age of fifty-nine she opened Sofreh restaurant in Brooklyn, to share the food and warm culture of her native Iran with a wider circle.

Now, in her first cookbook, Alikhani offers her readers what she has lovingly been providing for those who know her and who eat in her restaurant: the true tastes of Iran. Here is the timeless, soul-satisfying food of Persia, with its trademark bold herb and spice flavors, succulent, savory stews and stuffed meats, vast bounty of brightly pickled vegetables and fresh fruits, and much, much more.

Containing more than 120 recipes, Sofreh brings together traditional Iranian dishes and modern Sofreh favorites.

* Sour Cherry Rice
* Roasted Cauliflower with Shallot Yogurt and Pistachios
* Sour Chicken Stew
* Rosewater and Cardamom Custard
* and, of course, everything you need to create a true Iranian breakfast spread at home

A joyous celebration of one of the world’s great cuisines, this essential guide will delight home cooks everywhere.

title: When Software Becomes Cheap, Strategy Becomes Everything
description: AI is making software cheap to build. The companies pulling ahead are not the ones adding chatbots. They are rethinking their business models. Abundance reshapes product work more than scarcity ever d
fullDescription:
AI is making software cheap to build. The companies pulling ahead are not the ones adding chatbots. They are rethinking their business models. Abundance reshapes product work more than scarcity ever did. Intercom moved to outcome-based pricing and grew $100M+ in ARR. Kive scrapped its entire product category and rebuilt around generative AI. Both succeeded at the strategy layer, not the feature layer. When teams can execute faster than ever, strategic clarity is what separates velocity from wasted motion. This session is for product leaders ready to stop asking "where do we add AI?" and start asking "which problems are worth solving now?"

**Key Takeaways**

1. Why cheaper software creates more demand, not less work.
2. How to apply AI at the strategy level.
3. Ways to tell when AI changes your business model, not just your backlog
4. Techniques for maintaining strategic clarity when execution outpaces direction

title: Goat Hill & Bernal Heights Hike (Dogs Welcome)
description: We start with a brief hike through Glen Canyon Valley Park, 60 acres of wilderness, formerly referred to as the San Miguel Hills, located in the Glen Park neighborhood. Your hike will take you through
fullDescription:
We start with a brief hike through Glen Canyon Valley Park, 60 acres of wilderness, formerly referred to as the San Miguel Hills, located in the Glen Park neighborhood. Your hike will take you through the valley and woods, with dramatic rock (chert) formations, and Islais Creek, one of the few remaining free-flowing creeks in San Francisco. Then enter Billy goat Hill, what remains of a 1800’s quarry, named for the goats that grazed on its steep hillside. The Hill overlooks amazing views of San Francisco skyline on a clear day beautiful views of the Eastbay. After meandering our way to Dunkin hill and down through Church Street and part of mission we climb up to the top of Bernal Heights Hill for grand views of the city and Bay. We go up the steepest street in the world 41% grade Bradford street, Starting from Tompkins Avenue at 20% grade, after 150 feet, the slope doubles.

**Level:** Intermediate
**Total Distance:** 9.2 miles loop (steep sections)
**Hiking Time:** 3.5 -4 hours
**Elevation:** 475 feet Bernal Heights Hill, 300 in Glen Canyon, 41% in a spot
**Information:** Free Parking Lot across from Glen Park Bart Station and Street Parking, Dogs on leash, Restroom and water at Glen Park Rec Center at Elk Street & Chenery Street, San Francisco, CA 9412

**GPS:** Latitude: N37.739261° Longitude: W -122.457783°

**Map:** (SFMTA – includes all City Transit information)
https://www.sfmta.com/sites/default/files/pressreleases/2015/Muni%20Map%202015.pdf

**Trailhead:** Starting point, Parking Lot across from Glen Park Bart Station, Bosworth Street. BART - Glen Park Station 2901 Diamond Street, San Francisco, CA 94131

trail, at end cross street and continue on Paradise Ave straight, Right on Elk Street, Left into Glen Park Entrance **(Elk Street & Chenery Street), (Restroom Break)**, take the right path after Glen Park Rec, at the fork (With Rock formation on the hill, Right uphill, then but the first they are us continue right up the stairs and then left of the stairs into Tight path around George Christopher right trail into Goat Hill Trail **(Take group pic at observation platform),** then continue down the hill left on 30th St. right down hidden stairwell onto Castro’s Street continue on Castro Street Heights Boulevard Trail, Exit cross street Left uphill Folsom Street uphill steeps steps into Bernal Height, stay Left continue up to the top break lunch break at Rock Outcrop, then continue around title: Concert -->> Timeless Egypt: Egypt's Legacy in Motion
description: Music in-Takht, a wonderful Bay Area-based Egyptian heritage ensemble that wowed Meetup members at last year's SF International Arts Festival, returns to the Mission District's Community Music Center
fullDescription:
Music in-Takht, a wonderful Bay Area-based Egyptian heritage ensemble that wowed Meetup members at last year's SF International Arts Festival, returns to the Mission District's Community Music Center for another Arts Fest concert - this one featuring four world premieres by band members! The band's music selections for the evening will range from classical Egyptian music to contemporary Egyptian fusions with jazz, Irish, and Western classical music.

I believe the core musicians will be the same as at last year's concert (plus a guest artist this time) :

**Violinist and Music Director:** Basma Edrees
**Oud:** Matthew Wright
**Nay:** Seif Ibrahim
**Egyptian percussion and Drumset:** Mohamed Abdelradi
**Egyptian percussion:** Loay Dahbour
**Bass:** Ernesto Mazar Kindelan
**Keyboards:** Daniel Filip
**Harp:** Destiny Muhammad (guest artist)

CONCERT COST: Ticket prices for this general-admission-seating event are about $23 to $33, *depending on when you buy your tickets.*

***NOTE: The Early Bird ticket price of about $23 is available through March 31.***

I also request a one dollar donation from Meetup RSVPers who can afford it, to defray the hefty fees that Meetup charges me as lead organizer.

TICKETS/SEATING: **Buy your own tickets.** General admission seating.

TICKET LINK:
https://sfiaf.vbotickets.com/event/Timeless_Egypt_Egypts_Legacy_in_Motion_SF_International_Arts_Festival/183819

WHEN/WHERE TO MEET: Unless you're joining me for the optional pre-show dinner, let's meet at the doors to the Community Music Center at 544 Capp Street **no later than 6:30 p.m.** (when the doors open) so we can increase our chances of getting good seats together. The show starts at 7:00 p.m. and I expect it to consist of two sets (with an intermission), ending at 9:00 p.m.

HOW TO FIND ME: I'm WF, about 5 feet tall, with short salt-and-pepper hair and glasses. I'll send my mobile number to RSVPers prior to the show. **If I'm missing anyone when I get seated, I'll post a Comment on the Meetup Event Page with my location.** PLEASE TEXT ME IF YOU DON'T SPOT ME.

TRANSIT/LOCATION: Located south of Mission Street, between 20th and 21st Street, a convenient walk from 24th Street BART and Mission Street bus lines. If you're driving, allow extra time for street parking.

OPTIONAL PRE-SHOW DINNER: At 5:00 p.m. at Arabian Nights, located on Mission near 20th Street. *Dinner headcount is limited to SIX people, with preference given to Meetup members who don't flake out on their RSVPs.* (I will gladly accommodate additional people for the concert by itself.)

MORE INFO:

fullDescription:
Today we are going to watch the award-winning short film "**Night ride**" and discuss:

* **When do we cross the line from self-preservation to moral responsibility?**
* **Is passive compliance a form of complicity?**
**Night ride** is written and directed by Eirik Tveiten. It was released in 2020 and gained significant recognition, including an Academy Award nomination for Best Live Action Short Film at the 2023 Oscars and Tribeca 2022 as the Best Narrative Short.

**What's the plan?**

title: Astrophotography 101: Intro to Milky Way Astrophotography
description: Want to learn how to take pictures of the Milky Way? Ever wanted to learn how amazing starry night landscape images are made? Whether you have specific questions about your own astrophotography proces
fullDescription:
Want to learn how to take pictures of the Milky Way? Ever wanted to learn how amazing starry night landscape images are made? Whether you have specific questions about your own astrophotography process, or you are completely new to night photography, this workshop will provide one-on-one training to help you on your journey in taking your own night sky photography.

In this workshop, we will focus specifically on Milky Way photography using a dslr (or mirrorless) camera. Using equipment provided by Chabot, we will walk you through different techniques of capturing the night sky outside on our observation deck and work with specific software to process a finished photo. Feel free to bring your own equipment or images to process.

You will be trained on how to operate astronomy gear that you can rent through the [East Bay Astronomical Society’s](https://eastbayastro.org/) Loaner Scope Program and gain introductory knowledge of how to get started capturing images of the night sky.

This workshop is part of a 3-part series of introductory astrophotography 101 workshops that can be taken together or independently. Workshops will have a combination of classroom time and time outside taking pictures of the night sky. All equipment and laptops are provided.

Note - this is a ticketed, paid workshop. Cost is $75, or $60 for Chabot members. Please purchase a ticket and pay through [Chabot's website.](https://chabotspace.org/calendar/astrophotography-101-intro-to-milky-way-astrophotography/)
group: CDARC - California Dark Sky Astronomy Research Community

description:
THIS IS AN IN-PERSON MEETUP!

**We continue with our rotation of playing from only one of the three Daily Ukulele Books each month. This month we'll return to the original Daily Ukulele book (the Yell
fullDescription:
THIS IS AN IN-PERSON MEETUP!

**We continue with our rotation of playing from only one of the three Daily Ukulele Books each month. This month we'll return to the original Daily Ukulele book (the Yellow Book).**

If you'd like to play a song that isn't in the Yellow Book, please let me know in advance so I can post a link to the song chart on our meetup page, and please bring additional copies with you.

If you don’t have the Yellow, Blue, or Purple Books yet, you can purchase them from Jim Beloff’s website [https://www.fleamarketmusic.com/store/scripts/prodList.asp?idCategory=24].
You can also find them on amazon.com and other online bookstores, on eBay, and at local music shops and bookstores. Please bring your instrument(s), a floor or table music stand, your singing voices, and your fabulous ukulele spirit!

We'll play music from **5:00 pm to 7:30 pm**, with a short intermission.

We'll go around the room so that everyone can pick songs to play. If you pick a song, you will be encouraged (but not required) to lead it. If you'd like to let the group know in advance which songs you plan to select, please post the songs you choose in the comments, and we'll play one of those songs each time it's your turn to pick a song.

The dropbox link to The Daily Ukulele.
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/uca82l70r9a4hgjqohsgq/Daily_ukulele_yellow_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=44gm13r5gbvhstiv7ibnuojt1&st=dvrcg7n4&dl=0
The dropbox link to the Daily Ukulele - Leap Year Edition:
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/hy9pnffip1lc1rxs5zc8q/Daily_Ukulele_blue_Leap_Year_LINKED_v1.1.pdf?rlkey=oug9yq9ezckyly3ekj3su539u&st=2hgndbig&dl=0
The Dropbox link to the Groovy Song Book used in the Bronco Billy Zoom jams:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/ezywmwbck8229gu/2021-05-22_v9_Official%20BBJ%20Virtual%20Song%20Packet.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/ezywmwbck8229gu/2021-05-22_v9_Official%20BBJ%20Virtual%20Song%20Packet.pdf?dl=0)

A voluntary donation of $5 to defray Meetup website costs and to tip our hosts is appreciated. San Francisco Ukulele Rebellion logo kazoos will be available for purchase.

**IMPORTANT NOTICE:**
**I do not disclose the location of our in-person jams on the Meetup website. While the date and time of these in-person meetups will still be posted here, I'll be notifying people of the location of these meetups using an email distribution list. *If you'd like to be on this list, please email me at* sfukerebellion@yahoo.com *from the email address you'd like me to add to the distribution list.***

***COVID UPDATE***: Covid and the flu surged this winter. I think it's safe for us to meet, but ***I strongly encourage people to observe social distancing and other measures, like masking, that have been shown to inhibit the spread of airborne viruses like Covid and the flu***. For the safety of others in the group, ***please don't attend this meetup if you are not feeling well or if you are experiencing Covid-like or flu-like symptoms***. I won't require people to test for Covid, but ***I strongly encourage you to test for Covid on Saturday afternoon***. From what I understand, most of the people I know who have gotten Covid recently got it from someone who didn't even know they had it at the time of exposure. Thank you in advance for your thoughtfulness.

title: Bay 2 Breakers 2026
description:
**Even if you’ve never run a race, you’ve probably heard of Bay to Breakers!**
This iconic San Francisco tradition is a 12K (7.4 mile) run/walk/party with more than 25,000 participants. The course beg
fullDescription:
**Even if you’ve never run a race, you’ve probably heard of Bay to Breakers!**
This iconic San Francisco tradition is a 12K (7.4 mile) run/walk/party with more than 25,000 participants. The course begins near the Bay Bridge, climbs the famous Hayes Street Hill, winds through Golden Gate Park, and finishes near the historic Cliff House overlooking the breakers of the Pacific Ocean.

Bay to Breakers has a little bit of everything: elite runners, weekend warriors, creative “centipede” teams who run the course linked together, a group that starts at the finish line and runs *backward* to the start dressed like salmon, Elvis impersonators, tutus, outrageous costumes—and plenty of people just enjoying the fun atmosphere.

You’re welcome to **run, walk, or simply come cheer with us!**
• If you'd like to participate in the race, check out the registration link below.
• If you prefer to spectate, join our group to cheer on the runners and enjoy the festive energy together.
Please **RSVP** and let us know whether you plan to run/walk or just watch.
The race begins at **8:00 AM**, and parking can be challenging, so **carpooling or public transit is highly recommended.**

**\*\*\* LIABILITY WAIVER:**
By signing up for the running/walking event, you hereby release its organizers, hosts, and participants from any and all liability, claims, demands or causes of action that you now have, or may have in the future, for illness, injuries, damages, and/or economic loss of any kind or nature, arising in whole or in part out of the activities, advice, or information contained herein. You agree to full responsibility for your own health issues.

REGISTRATION
[Bay 2 Breakers Reservation](https://www.baytobreakers.com)

SAN FRANCISCO TRAVEL ARTICLE
[San Francisco Travel](https://www.sftravel.com/article/everything-you-need-to-know-about-bay-to-breakers)
group: South Bay Indoor / Outdoor Activities Group (SBIO)

title: SF Bay to Breakers 12k/15k party-run
description: NOTE: this is more of an informational event posting and less of a typical Meetup event posting since this is a city event with thousands of people, so meeting specific people from our group will be h
fullDescription:
NOTE: this is more of an informational event posting and less of a typical Meetup event posting since this is a city event with thousands of people, so meeting specific people from our group will be hard. Making this event anyways though as a way to get the word out to people who might be new to the area and don't know about this, and also leaving the comments open to anyone who wants to try coordinating something.

If you're new to the Bay Area, come check [out this annual 12k run/walk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bay_to_Breakers) in San Francisco. Thousands of people attend this event, and this race even set the world record for most participants in a footrace with 110,000 participants back in 1986! This is a pretty laid back event, with many people dressing up in costumes for the race, so don't worry if you're not a runner since there will be plenty of us walking too. **This is less of a race and more of a party where people eat, drink, and play music along the course, so this is a fun time!** [Here's another article](https://www.theownteam.com/blog/bay-to-breakers-2025-san-franciscos-iconic-run-returns/) discussing the event.

There's the traditional 12k race, an extended 15k race, and a centipede version where you're tied together with (up to) 12 other people and you need to complete the race together (links to all of these below).
The first wave starts at 8:00am and last wave starts at 8:45am (from Main Street and Howard Street near Embarcadero).

Also keep in mind **prices increase as we get closer to the event, so get your ticket now if you know you want to go!**

Event links:
Registration: https://endurancecui.active.com/event-reg/select-race?e=94447218
[https://www.baytobreakers.com/12k](https://www.baytobreakers.com/12)
[https://www.baytobreakers.com/15k](https://www.baytobreakers.com/15k)
[https://www.baytobreakers.com/centipede](https://www.baytobreakers.com/centipede)

General:
[https://www.baytobreakers.com/](https://www.baytobreakers.com/)
[https://www.baytobreakers.com/eventschedule](https://www.baytobreakers.com/eventschedule)

FAQs:
[https://motivsports1.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/categories/14024414239771-Bay-to-Breakers](https://motivsports1.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/categories/14024414239771-Bay-to-Breakers)
**NOTE again in case you skipped the start**: since there are thousands of people at this event and people might be in different heats, meeting before (and probably during) the event will be chaos, ***so this will not be a typical Meetup experience.*** If you want to run with other people, I encourage you to come out to other events we host so you can meet people in person and figure out who you want to run with.

**SAFETY DISCLAIMER**
The organizers of this meet-up are unpaid volunteers and not expert guides. All participants agree to assume 100% responsibility for their own safety, conduct, and well-being and recognize that possible hazards exist while participating in outdoor (or any other) activities, and agree to participate solely at their own risk. If you RSVP “Yes” and/or attend any of our events, it shall be conclusively presumed that you understand and agree to this policy.

title: Salad for President: A Cookbook for Artists
description:
For our May meetup, we'll be cooking from Salad for President, by Julia Sherman.

We’ll be meeting at Susie's home in Rockridge (Oakland/Berkeley). Please bring at least one recipe’s worth of food, pr
fullDescription:
For our May meetup, we'll be cooking from Salad for President, by Julia Sherman.

We’ll be meeting at Susie's home in Rockridge (Oakland/Berkeley). Please bring at least one recipe’s worth of food, prepared for 6 to 8 people.

NOTE: Earlier 5pm start time for this event only.

**NEW POLICY: Please post your recipe in the comments before Wednesday, May 13, to keep your spot at the table. You'll be moved to "Not Going" if you don't post. You'll receive the address on Wednesday if you do post your dish.**

**As always, no shows and cancellations will get you removed from the group. Be aware of any allergens in your dish and substitute if possible.**

Julia Sherman loves salad. In the book named for her popular blog, she encourages her readers to consider salad an everyday indulgence that can include cocktails, soups, family-style brunch dishes, and dinner-party entrées. Every part of the meal is reimagined with a fresh, vegetable-obsessed perspective.
This compendium of savory recipes will tempt readers in search of diverse offerings from light to hearty organized by season. Recipes include:

* Collard Chiffonade Salad with Roasted Garlic Dressing and Crouton Crumble
* Heirloom Tomatoes with Crunchy Polenta Croutons
* Flank Steak and Bean Sprouts with Miso-Kimchi Dressing
* Grilled Hearts of Palm with Mint and Triple Citrus
* Golden Crispy Lotus Root with Asian Pear and Yuzu Dressing
* Shaved Cauliflower and Candy Cane Beet Salad with Seared Arctic Char
* Curly Carrots with Candied Cumin
* And many more!

The recipes, while not exclusively vegetarian, are vegetable-forward and focused on high-quality seasonal produce. Sherman also includes insider tips on pantry staples and growing your own salad garden of herbs and greens. Salad―with its infinite possibilities―is a game of endless combinations, not stifling rules.
And with that in mind, Salad for President offers a window into how artists approach preparing their favorite dishes. She visits sculptors, painters, photographers, and musicians in their homes and gardens, interviewing and photographing them as they cook.
Utterly unique in its look into the worlds of food, art, and everyday practices, Salad for President is at once a practical resource for healthy, satisfying recipes and an inspiring look at creativity.

title: Exploring Patagonia: An Introductory Guide to Traveling in Torres del Paine
description: Join us for an informative and inspiring presentation all about Chile's Torres del Paine, one of the most breathtaking national parks in Patagonia! Whether you're planning your first trip or just curi
fullDescription:
Join us for an informative and inspiring presentation all about Chile's Torres del Paine, one of the most breathtaking national parks in Patagonia! Whether you're planning your first trip or just curious about what it takes to explore this stunning region, this session will cover everything you need to know about traveling to this iconic part of the world.

I'll be drawing from my own Torres del Paine adventure from 2023 to discuss practical details like logistics, safety, and main things to keep in mind when going on this once-in-a-lifetime adventure. The presentation itself will be 30-40 minutes, after which we'll have a casual Q&A session for anything anyone wants to cover and chat for a while. Join and share some of your own travel stories! (Would love to hear from people who've done Camino de Santiago, the Pacific Crest Trail, Hornbjarg in Iceland, etc.)

Anyone is welcome, but please note that - given the limited space - this event is geared mainly for people who are actively interested in these kinds of travels and would benefit the most from the information shared.

Also, please note the location will be my apartment, and that I live close to the Crepevine location listed in Meetup. I'll share my address to those attending about a week before the event.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions. 628-236-9369

title: 💚Green Exercise🌻🌷🌸Wildflowers 🌊Ocean Hike 🥾Beautiful Mori Point Sunset🌄
description:
**Hello Ocean Lovers!**
Ready to trade the treadmill for ocean breezes? 🌊 Let’s gather for a 💚“green exercise” wellness Hike, Rock climbing/hiking/walking at the Rockaway Beach🏖️ to the scenic Mori
fullDescription:
**Hello Ocean Lovers!**
Ready to trade the treadmill for ocean breezes? 🌊 Let’s gather for a 💚“green exercise” wellness Hike, Rock climbing/hiking/walking at the Rockaway Beach🏖️ to the scenic Mori Point Trail! Fresh sea air, breathtaking views, and the soothing sounds of the waves await.🌞 Come make new friends, recharge your body, and let the healing magic of the ocean lift your spirit. Because nothing beats a little seaside therapy for our physical health and mental well-being! 😇

Rockaway Beach is located Pacifica, CA that offers scenic views. "This is a great place to hike and to enjoy the great views of the Pacifica coastline. There are trails that hug the coastline climb up to the hilltop for panoramic views."

MORI POINT
Spectacular 360 degrees ocean view. This place has a lot of that here. And most of all, it also has an impeccable view of the sunset. Take advantage of the free sunset show only at Mori Point.

ROCKAWAY BEACH
Watch the sun drop below the horizon at Rockaway Beach. This hidden gem in the Pacifica is a sight to behold. You can learn surfing while waiting for the sun to set or enjoy your afternoon snack at a nearby restaurant while waiting for the sun to KISS the OCEAN.

💚 Support Our Group!
Thanks for joining our wellness club event, it does take time and money to make it happen. Thank you for being prepared and supportive 🌿
Our hikes, dinners, and events run thanks to member support. A small contribution is appreciated to help keep our wellness community active and fun! Thank you for helping us make every event special!

**\*Requested contribution** suggested amount $5-$20 to keep the club going!
**​Dr. Heidi will ask for the contribution during event check-in.** Accepted in person. Your support helps cover the many behind-the-scenes efforts that make these events possible, including route scouting, Meetup platform fees, permits, communication with park staff and members, and planning hikes, dinners, and road trips.
This community exists because of the support and kindness of its members.

**The organizer reserves the right to ask a participant to depart!**
If they refuse to contribute or follow event guidelines. This ensures our community remains a positive and respectful space for everyone.
Thank you for helping keep our wellness community positive, respectful, and active. 🌿

Our meetup events have a very positive impact on people's lives, people meet, fall in love💘 meet new friends, HAPPY, better health, less stress, anxiety, depression, isolate being alone and got extra bonus for LOVE ​💞

⚠️ Please Read Before Joining:
These hikes and group dinners are hosted voluntarily and with love for the outdoors and community. Participation is completely voluntary, and everyone joins at their own risk and responsibility.
👉 We kindly ask you to review the event details carefully — including directions, trail descriptions, and dinner spots — before joining.
Trails are part of nature and may not match everyone’s preferences. Dinner venues are selected based on group size, availability, and location. We do our best but can’t guarantee personal favorites.
🧡 Most importantly, come with an open heart, positive energy, and kindness. Let’s enjoy what we can control — good company, fresh Ocean air, and meaningful connections.

**Meet time: 1:30pm** & **Hike time: 2pm**
Meet by beach parking lot near the big rocks
📍GPS: https://maps.app.goo.gl/fJnCgTYjDQadaEzo7
📍Address: Parking lot, 651 San Marlo Way, Pacifica, CA 94044

Level: Moderate + challenging! several Steep Hills climbs...
Total Distance: 5-6 miles
Hiking Time: 2.5-3 hours
Elevation: 800 ft
Route: Loop
Parking: Free
Dogs: No!
Exposure: Sun
Views: Amazingly Beautiful

**Bring Smiles for Miles**! Water, Snacks, Sunscreens, Hiking Poles
Wear Sturdy Hiking shoes
Near Ocean can be windy and/or foggy, so wear layers

Encouraging carpooling if you know each other well for everyone's safety. Please contact each other in a private message not on the message board. Thank you! https://www.groupcarpool.com/t/sy02q5

Optional: Sunset time around 6:30pm to 7pm
After hiking the social dinner place TBD...

\*We are an intermediate hiking group. Please be ready to keep up hiking with us from 5-6 miles without any health issues. If you don't workout, afraid high, steepest hill climbs mountains, unable to hiking minimum 5 miles regularly, this event is not a good fit for you.

**\*LIABILITY WAIVER**: By signing up for the wellness hiking event, you hereby release its organizers, hosts, and participants from any and all liability, claims, demands or causes of action that you now have, or may have in the future, for illness, injuries, damages, and/or economic loss of any kind or nature, arising in whole or in part out of the activities, advice, or information contained herein. You agree to full responsibility for your own health issues.

**\*DISCLAIMER:** By participating in this wellness hiking event, you understand that Meetup event Host/Organizer assumes ABSOLUTELY NO responsibility and you have to assume all at your own risk of injury, damage, and economic loss, without limitation, and release host/Organizer of all liability.

**\*Hike at YOUR OWN RISK**. Open trails aren't necessarily free of hazards--by entering the wilderness, you assume responsibility for your safety and must use good judgment.

**\*Participation in this event is strictly voluntary!** The event host/organizer assumes no responsibility for any injury, damage, or loss you may incur. You join and hike entirely at your own risk.

**\*DO NOT ATTEND** if you're experiencing any cold or flu symptoms, have a compromised immune system.

If anyone wishes to join our hike team, please answer the RSVP question every time when you sign up for our event. For everyone's safety in the group. If you are unable to send me a message confirming that you are in good health and you are able to hike 5-6 miles without health issues we CANNOT approve you come to this hiking event.
\*Please don't just walk in. Thank you for your cooperation!

I'm looking forward to meeting, hiking with you and making good memories together.
Yours in good health! Dr. Heidi💝

❤️American Heart Association BLS-CPR, Heartsaver CPR, First Aid Courses -Learn and Live!❤️
\*Three courses available upon request:
[https://visibook.com/ahacprfirstaid](https://visibook.com/ahacprfirstaid)
\*By taking this course, you're contributing to the safety and well-being of those around you.
\*These skills are crucial for everyone to know, as they can help save the lives of loved ones, friends, or even strangers in the community.
